acceptance: Zero-config back-compat: a warren deploy with no `[workers]` block and existing `WARREN_BURROW_*` env vars behaves identically to today's single-worker container — same socket, same dispatch flow, no behavior change (verified by existing handlers.test.ts / scheduler.test.ts / bridges.test.ts passing unmodified).
Warren with two workers in `[workers]` config dispatches two runs for the same `projectId` to the same worker (project-affinity); a run for a different `projectId` round-robins.
`POST /workers/A/drain` on warren issues `/admin/drain` to worker A, flips warren's state to `draining`, and the next dispatch lands on worker B; orphaned runs on A continue to terminal state.

Warren's `GET /burrows` (fan-out) returns the union of burrows from both workers, sorted by createdAt, with worker_unreachable log lines on any rejected fan-out (verified by killing one worker mid-list).
Sticky-by-burrow holds: a `runs.create` against an existing burrow on an unreachable worker fails with a structured error, NOT a silent migration.
warren SPEC §3.2's `No remote burrow workers` line is removed; warren SPEC §5.4 `Multi-worker model` exists and cross-links burrow-62ce.
TOML config loader: empty file → identical to env-only; populated file → values merged; env override → env wins; malformed TOML → structured ValidationError.
`BURROW_API_TOKEN` required when `[workers]` is non-empty; warren refuses to start otherwise (surfaced via `warren doctor`).
Two-worker integration test passes: affinity, failover-on-drain, fan-out union all green.
`bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` clean on warren.

Joint release (covered by the burrow plan's final step) ships a minor bump on both repos together; warren CHANGELOG entry notes the worker pool, `[workers]` config, the §3.2 non-goal removal, and the new admin endpoints.

sections.context: Warren seeds burrow workspaces by writing directly to burrow.workspacePath on disk (src/runs/seed.ts) and reaps mulch records / closed seeds by reading directly off disk (src/runs/reap.ts). This is only viable because warren and burrow are co-tenanted in one container sharing /data — every other warren↔burrow interaction is HTTP with a typed contract; these two reach past the seam onto disk. Warren's own SPEC §11.A acknowledges the gap ("invokes ml record inside the burrow workspace via burrow exec or equivalent"); the equivalent has now shipped. Burrow's R-07 (plan pl-2467, closed 2026-05-09) delivered the workspace-mutation HTTP surface: POST /burrows with an atomic seed payload, POST /burrows/:id/files for post-provision writes, GET /burrows/:id/files for reads. Warren's existing dep @os-eco/burrow-cli ^0.2.12 already exposes HttpClient.burrows.create({ seed }), HttpClient.files.write, HttpClient.files.read. Closing the seam violation is the warren-side half of R-07 and the prerequisite for any future remote-burrow topology where shared disk is gone.
sections.approach: Refactor seedBurrowWorkspace from a side-effecting writer into a pure builder returning HttpWorkspaceFile[] (path + contents + optional encoding/mode). Thread the produced list into burrows.up via the new HttpClient seed parameter so provisioning + seeding become a single atomic round-trip (failed seed rolls the burrow back, contract guaranteed by burrow). For reap: migrate seeds_close (single known file .seeds/issues.jsonl) to HttpClient.files.read immediately; defer mulch_merge until burrow ships a list endpoint (no R-07 file-listing surface exists today, and reap currently relies on readdir(.mulch/expertise/) to discover domains). Branch_push is fundamentally workspace-local — explicitly out of scope. Delete the SeedFs injection seam and disk-writing helpers; tests switch from fake-fs assertions to asserting on the produced HttpWorkspaceFile[] shape and on HttpClient mock call args. Chosen over alternatives because the atomic provision+seed contract is already proven on the burrow side and the builder refactor is mechanical: every existing seed.ts write path maps 1:1 to a file entry.

alternatives.name: Two-call approach: burrows.up then HttpClient.files.write
alternatives.rejected_because: Extra round-trip with no benefit, and not atomic — a failure after up but before files.write leaves a half-seeded burrow that's already visible to the dispatcher. R-07 specifically ships the provision-time seed payload to avoid this; not using it would be malpractice.

alternatives.name: Big-bang reap migration (block seed work on list-files endpoint)
alternatives.rejected_because: The seam violation is fixed on the spawn path the moment seed.ts stops touching disk — that's the load-bearing win. mulch_merge needs a burrow-side list endpoint that doesn't exist yet; pinning seed migration to that timeline gives up the immediate fix for a downstream-blocked piece. Split the work.

alternatives.name: Self-seeding agents (agent definitions ship their own seed bootstrap)
alternatives.rejected_because: Already rejected during burrow R-07's design discussion (plan pl-2467 alternative #3) — pushes persistence mechanics into every agent definition, couples agents to storage details, breaks the canopy/mulch separation. The HttpClient seed surface is the agreed contract; warren is the consumer.

steps.title: Refactor seedBurrowWorkspace into a pure HttpWorkspaceFile[] builder
steps.description: Rename the side-effecting seedBurrowWorkspace to buildSeedFiles (or similar) in src/runs/seed.ts. Each write helper (writeCanopyAgent, writeExpertiseSeed, writeWorkflowTemplate, writePiArtifacts) becomes a pure function returning HttpWorkspaceFile entries instead of calling fs.writeFile/appendFile. Remove SeedFs and the fs injection seam. Update seed.test.ts to assert on the returned file list (shape, count, ordering) instead of stubbing a fake fs. Keep the existing validation (JSON shape, domain checks, isSafeArtifactName) — those now run inside the pure builder. Encoding defaults to 'utf-8' for all current artifacts; binary support is N/A for the V1 consumer set. Re-export the file shape from src/runs/index.ts if any consumer needs it.

steps.title: Thread seed payload through provisionBurrow → burrows.up
steps.description: In src/runs/spawn.ts, call buildSeedFiles(agent) before provisionBurrow and pass the result through as seed: { files }. Update HttpBurrowUpInput signature accordingly (it already accepts seed via burrow-cli ^0.2.12, just confirm the type lines up). Move the seed step BEFORE the burrows.up call atomically — provisionBurrow becomes the single seam where workspace bytes land. Delete the standalone seedFn call after up, the seedWorkspace injection seam, and the RunSpawnError wrapper for seed-failure (the burrow rollback contract makes a failed seed a failed up, no separate failure mode). Update spawn.test.ts: existing tests that asserted on seedFn invocation switch to asserting on the burrowClient.http.burrows.up mock args, including the seed.files payload.

steps.title: Migrate reap seeds_close to HttpClient.files.read
steps.description: In src/runs/reap.ts, replace the fs.readFile(.seeds/issues.jsonl) call inside mirrorClosedSeeds with input.burrowClient.http.files.read({ burrowId, path: '.seeds/issues.jsonl' }). NotFoundError (file absent) maps to the same null-body branch the disk path uses today. The write side (.seeds/issues.jsonl on the PROJECT clone, not the burrow workspace) stays a local-disk operation — that's project-side state, not a seam violation. Update reap.test.ts: the ReapFs.readFile stub for the burrow-workspace .seeds/issues.jsonl path migrates to a burrowClient.http.files.read mock. Branch_push and project-clone writes stay disk-bound for now and are noted as follow-ups (see step 6).

steps.title: File burrow follow-up: list files endpoint
steps.description: Reap's mulch_merge currently fs.readdir(.mulch/expertise/) to discover which domain JSONL files the agent recorded — no list endpoint exists in R-07. File a burrow seed for either GET /burrows/:id/files?prefix=.mulch/expertise/ (returns matching paths) or a separate GET /burrows/:id/files/list?dir=… shape. Decide which surface during that seed's design; reap's needs are: list relative paths under a given prefix, no recursion required for V1. Once burrow ships that, file the follow-up warren seed ("Migrate reap mulch_merge to HttpClient.files.read + list") and link it back to warren-0a83 via --relates-to. This step's deliverable is the burrow seed ID — no warren code changes.

steps.title: SPEC + CHANGELOG + version bump
steps.description: Update warren SPEC §11.A: replace 'invokes ml record inside the burrow workspace via burrow exec (or equivalent)' with the concrete HTTP contract reference (HttpClient.burrows.up with seed + HttpClient.files.read). Note the reap mulch_merge gap and link the burrow follow-up seed from step 4. Add CHANGELOG.md entry under a new 0.2.0 heading: 'R-07 adoption — warren seeds and reaps burrow workspaces over HTTP; the SeedFs disk-writing path is removed (links burrow plan pl-2467).' Bump warren/package.json from 0.1.7 to 0.2.0 (minor bump — the public CLI/HTTP surface is unchanged but the internal seam contract is a meaningful break for anyone forking spawn.ts). Run bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck.

risks: Seed-payload size limits — burrow's V1 doesn't enforce a per-call quota, but JSON+base64 inflates wire size ~33%. Warren's known seed payloads (canopy agent JSON, expertise JSONL, workflow.txt, pi skills/prompts) are all small text; verify the largest realistic payload fits under any HTTP body cap in src/server/handlers.ts readJsonBody. If a cap exists, follow-up: split large pi_skills sections across post-provision files.write calls.

Atomic-seed failure surfaces differently than disk-seed failure — today a seed failure throws RunSpawnError mid-spawn against a partially-provisioned burrow that warren's rollback destroys. R-07's atomic contract rolls the burrow back BEFORE the error reaches warren (the up call itself rejects). Update the spawn rollback path: burrow is null when up fails, error message surfaces burrow's seed-validation message verbatim. Lock the new failure shape in a spawn.test.ts case.

Test churn — SeedFs stub removal touches seed.test.ts and spawn.test.ts (main consumers; global search for SeedFs / seedWorkspace shows the scope). Mitigate by keeping the new tests structurally similar (same case count, same scenario names) so review can diff cleanly.

Reap mulch_merge stays disk-bound until burrow ships a list endpoint — acceptance explicitly excludes mulch_merge migration; tracked by the burrow follow-up seed filed in step 4. Warren ROADMAP gets a one-liner so it doesn't slip; when the burrow endpoint lands, the follow-up warren seed picks up mulch_merge as a small mechanical change.
acceptance: src/runs/seed.ts contains no fs/promises imports; SeedFs interface and seedWorkspace injection seam are removed.

buildSeedFiles(agent) returns HttpWorkspaceFile[] covering the same five drops (canopy agent JSON, mulch expertise JSONL, seeds workflow.txt, pi skills SKILL.md, pi prompts .md) with identical content and same validation errors as the prior writer.
src/runs/spawn.ts threads buildSeedFiles output into burrowClient.http.burrows.up({ seed: { files } }); no separate seedFn call after provisionBurrow.
spawn.test.ts asserts on the seed.files payload passed to the burrows.up mock; a failure-injection test exercises the atomic-rollback failure shape end-to-end.
src/runs/reap.ts mirrorClosedSeeds uses input.burrowClient.http.files.read; reap.test.ts mocks files.read with success + NotFoundError cases.
Burrow follow-up seed for the list endpoint is filed and its ID is recorded in this seed's close comment.
CHANGELOG.md has a 0.2.0 entry referencing burrow plan pl-2467; package.json is bumped; bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck all pass.

outcomeNote: All 5 steps closed. R-07 disk-seam closure shipped on spawn (seed.files atomic provision-and-seed) and reap seeds_close (HttpClient.files.read). mulch_merge follow-up tracked as burrow-18ca (warren-7f7c). Warren 0.2.0.

sections.context: Today a successful warren run ends with a pushed branch and a reap summary. The reviewer's next step is git-checkout-and-run-locally, which is high-friction enough that most runs are judged on the diff alone. ROADMAP R-19 proposes that each successful run optionally exposes a public URL of the form `https://run-<id>.<warren-host>` that proxies into the burrow workspace's listening port, so runs feel like deployable artifacts rather than text diffs. ROADMAP files R-19 as [proposed] / needs-sketching: the sketch touches at least five layers (burrow inbound networking, warren proxy + routing, TLS / wildcard certs, auth on the preview URL, lifecycle + eviction) and the open questions across them (same-sandbox-vs-forked, auth scheme, port allocator, remote-worker interaction, non-HTTP fallback, failure UX) need to be locked before any code lands. Burrow's bwrap profiles bind outbound-only today (SPEC §5.3 / §7), so this work is cross-repo: a burrow-side networking-policy change is on the critical path. The plan starts with a SPEC §11.L design pass + a coordination seed against burrow, then ships the warren-side scaffolding (config schema, db columns, port allocator), the reap-time launch sub-step (mirroring pr_open's best-effort pattern, mx-05abb2), the host proxy + signed-cookie auth, the TTL / LRU eviction worker, the UI badge + teardown affordance, and an acceptance scenario covering the happy path + the eviction path. Default same-sandbox per ROADMAP's open-question note; static-site fallback rides on the proxy layer once routing exists.
sections.approach: Phase along the natural seam: lock the cross-repo design and burrow contract first, then ship the foundational layers (`.warren/defaults.json` schema, `runs` columns, port allocator) in parallel, then the reap-time launch sub-step that ties them together (mirroring pr_open: a best-effort 5th sub-step in `src/runs/reap.ts` that only fires on `outcome==="succeeded"` and never fails the run — pattern from mx-05abb2). The host proxy is a new in-process Bun route that matches `Host: run-<id>.<warren-host>`, resolves `runs.preview_port` → `127.0.0.1:<port>`, and forwards HTTP / WS through `burrowClientPool.clientFor({burrowId})` so the multi-worker placement (warren-c0c9 / pl-9ba1 step 5) keeps holding. Auth on the preview URL uses a signed cookie issued from `GET /runs/:id/preview/login?token=...&redirect=...` (option (a) in ROADMAP's open-question list) — bearer-in-header is impossible for a browser hitting `run-foo.host` directly. TLS termination stays on the operator's Caddy / Fly edge per SPEC §8.1; warren ships docs for the wildcard CNAME + DNS-01 wildcard cert with a Caddy snippet, not built-in cert provisioning. Lifecycle is a periodic eviction sweep that combines per-preview TTL with a global LRU cap (`WARREN_PREVIEW_MAX_LIVE`, default 20) so a long-tail of `bun run dev` sidecars can't exhaust container memory. Same sandbox as the agent run (not a fork) per ROADMAP's tradeoff note — document the side-effect risk in SPEC §11.L. Remote-worker (R-12) interaction is explicitly out of scope here: lock the design so the proxy URL → worker mapping uses the same denormalized `runs.worker_id` already in place, and defer cross-host routing to V2-of-this-item once R-12 wires real network topology. Each step's child seed is sized so it can be re-planned via `sd plan submit` when its turn arrives — the burrow-side change in particular is a coordination dep and gets its own forward-blocking edge so warren-side scaffolding can advance while burrow lands its half.

alternatives.name: Ship a single mega-PR with design + burrow change + warren code + UI + docs
alternatives.rejected_because: Cross-repo coordination, an unsettled design surface, and a load-bearing reap sub-step are exactly the shape that wants a phased plan with forward-blocking edges. A single trunk PR would be unreviewable and would couple the burrow timeline to warren's; phasing lets the burrow inbound-networking change ship and unblock warren step 6 on its own pace.

alternatives.name: Fork a fresh sandbox from the agent's final commit for the preview
alternatives.rejected_because: ROADMAP's open-question note already calls this out: forking doubles burrow workload and complicates port binding for marginal safety. Default same-sandbox per the ROADMAP tradeoff; document the side-effect risk in SPEC §11.L. Forked-sandbox can be a V2-of-this-item flag if a real operator hits a security incident.

alternatives.name: Skip the burrow-side change; expose the burrow port via host-namespace-only iptables
alternatives.rejected_because: Burrow's bwrap profile owns the network namespace — punching through with iptables on the host would skip burrow's policy layer and create a hidden trust boundary. ROADMAP §11.G / R-15 already flag the inbound-networking seam as burrow's concern. Coordinating one cross-repo change is cheaper than carrying a parallel host-side networking story forever.

alternatives.name: Auth via per-run basic-auth password surfaced in the reap summary (ROADMAP option c)
alternatives.rejected_because: Reviewer UX is bad — every link click is a credential prompt — and operators have to scrape the reap event log to find the password. The signed-cookie option (a) reuses the bearer token warren already trusts and degrades gracefully when the operator hasn't set up the preview path. Defer (b) GitHub OAuth to R-18 ship; (c)/(d) remain explicitly off the table by default.

alternatives.name: Eager-launch preview sidecars while the agent is still running
alternatives.rejected_because: Half the agent runs fail or get cancelled. Burning sidecar memory + a port allocation before terminal success means most allocations are wasted. The reap-time pattern (mx-05abb2) already established the right point in the lifecycle: only succeeded runs are worth previewing, and reap is the load-bearing transition that already runs project-aware post-processing.

alternatives.name: Warren terminates TLS and provisions wildcard certs itself
alternatives.rejected_because: SPEC §8.1 / §11.D locked TLS termination to the operator's reverse proxy (Caddy on home server, Fly edge on Fly.io). Cert provisioning is out of warren's scope; baking it in would force ACME / DNS-01 plumbing that doesn't fit warren's 'self-hostable control plane' framing. Ship operator docs with a Caddy wildcard snippet instead.

steps.title: Design lock — SPEC §11.L + burrow inbound-networking seed
steps.description: Add a §11.L design doc to SPEC.md that closes ROADMAP R-19's open questions: same-sandbox-default (with side-effect tradeoff documented), signed-cookie auth on the preview URL (option (a)), port allocator strategy (SQLite-backed `runs.preview_port`, default range 30000-31000), TTL + LRU eviction contract, failure UX (`preview_state='failed'` with last 200 lines of stdout/stderr, run still succeeded), and the deliberate R-12 deferral (remote-worker preview routing is out of scope for V1 of this item). File a coordination seed in `../burrow/.seeds/` for the inbound-networking policy: bwrap profile gains an opt-in port-forward (`127.0.0.1:<host-port>` → burrow's internal port) gated by a new `[sandbox].inbound_ports` array in burrow.toml, and the burrow HTTP API gains an endpoint (e.g. `POST /burrows/:id/exec-sidecar`) to spawn a long-lived process inside the same workspace sandbox with a declared port-forward. No warren code yet — this step is the gate.

steps.title: Burrow change — inbound networking policy + sidecar exec endpoint (cross-repo)
steps.description: Track the burrow-side change locked in step 1. Burrow ships: (a) `[sandbox].inbound_ports: [{external: <host-port>, internal: <bwrap-port>}]` in burrow.toml schema, (b) bwrap profile generation that forwards host loopback → bwrap net ns for the listed ports, (c) a new HTTP route to spawn a sidecar process inside the same workspace sandbox returning a handle (process id + stream subscription), (d) a route to kill the sidecar handle. Warren-side seed tracks the dep; the actual code lives in `../burrow/`. This step's child seed is a tracking pointer that closes when burrow merges + bumps the pinned `@os-eco/burrow-cli` version in warren's `package.json` + `bun.lock` + Dockerfile (per CLAUDE.md 'Relationship to burrow' — three places).

steps.title: Extend `.warren/defaults.json` schema with a `preview` block
steps.description: Add to `DefaultsConfigSchema` (src/warren-config/schema.ts) a new optional `preview: { enabled: boolean, command: string, port: number, readiness_path?: string, ttl?: string }` block. Mirror the R-02 posture (mx-f5b07a): parse here, consume in step 6. Reuse mx-3636de's discriminator-style design if future preview kinds (e.g. static-site directory) need siblings — leave room for a `kind: 'http' | 'static'` future-proof field while shipping only `http` today. Update the three lockstep places per mx-5339d5: ROADMAP R-02 schema docs, src/warren-config/schema.ts, the loader test. Loader stays missing-vs-malformed (mx-66d478): preview block absent ⇒ `preview: null`; preview malformed ⇒ surfaces in the per-file errors envelope.

steps.title: Add preview columns to the `runs` table + RunsRepo.attachPreview
steps.description: Migration 0009 adds: `preview_state TEXT` (nullable; enum `starting|live|torn-down|failed`), `preview_port INTEGER` (allocated host-loopback port; null until reap allocates), `preview_url TEXT` (the public URL once routed), `preview_started_at TEXT`, `preview_torn_down_at TEXT`, `preview_failure_message TEXT` (last 200 lines of sidecar stdout/stderr on `failed`). Update `src/db/schema.ts` PREVIEW_STATES const + `RunRow`. Add `RunsRepo.attachPreview(input: AttachPreviewInput)` mirroring `attachStats` partial-input semantics (mx-49272e) — omitted fields preserved, never silently overwritten. Index on `preview_state` for the eviction sweep (step 7). Tests cover: insert with all-null preview fields, attach starting → live → torn-down progression, attach failed with failure_message.

steps.title: Port allocator (SQLite-backed, restart-safe)
steps.description: New module `src/runs/preview-ports.ts` exposing `allocatePort(repos, range): Promise<number>` and `releasePort(repos, port): Promise<void>`. Reads `runs.preview_port WHERE preview_state IN ('starting','live')` to find in-use ports; picks the lowest free port in the configured range. Range defaults to 30000-31000, overridable via `WARREN_PREVIEW_PORT_RANGE='30000-31000'` (src/server-config / loadFromEnv pattern, mx-af4d39). Survives restart because the in-use set is derived from the durable `runs` table, not an in-memory map. Throws `NoFreePortError` when the range is exhausted (caller emits `preview_failed` event + sets state). Unit tests cover: empty range, partial occupation, full occupation, restart simulation (re-derive from runs table).

steps.title: Reap-time preview launch sub-step (5th best-effort step)
steps.description: Add `launchPreview` as the 5th best-effort sub-step in `src/runs/reap.ts`, mirroring `pr_open` (mx-05abb2). Only fires when `outcome === 'succeeded'`, project has `preview.enabled: true` in `.warren/defaults.json`, and the burrow inbound-networking change from step 2 is available (probe burrow `/healthz` for capability flag, or fall back to a feature-flag env var). Flow: (a) allocate port via step 5, (b) call `burrowClient.http.execSidecar(burrowId, {command, port, readinessPath})` (the new step-2 route), (c) poll `readiness_path` on `127.0.0.1:<allocated-port>` until 200 OK or timeout (default 60s), (d) `attachPreview({state:'live', port, url, startedAt})` and emit `preview_started` audit event. On any failure: `attachPreview({state:'failed', failure_message})` + emit `preview_failed`. Same-sandbox default per design lock (step 1); never throws — wrapped like every other reap sub-step (mx-05abb2). Tests use a fake `burrowClient` (mx-6779e5 FakeBurrowClientOpts pattern) to drive sidecar success + readiness-poll failure + port-exhaustion.

steps.title: TTL + LRU eviction worker
steps.description: New module `src/runs/preview-evict.ts` with a periodic sweeper that runs every `WARREN_PREVIEW_SWEEP_INTERVAL_MS` (default 60s). Two eviction triggers, evaluated in order: (a) per-preview TTL — any row with `preview_state IN ('starting','live')` whose `preview_started_at + ttl < now` is torn down; (b) global LRU cap — when count of `live` previews exceeds `WARREN_PREVIEW_MAX_LIVE` (default 20), oldest-by-`preview_started_at` are torn down until under cap. Teardown: call burrow's kill-sidecar route from step 2 via `burrowClientPool.clientFor({burrowId})`, then `attachPreview({state:'torn-down', torn_down_at})`. Emit `preview_evicted` audit event with reason `ttl` or `lru`. Bootstrapped from `src/supervisor/main.ts` after the warren process is up (mirroring mx-c01c8a's supervisor module structure). Tests inject a fake clock + fake burrow client and exercise both triggers + the ordering.

steps.title: Host reverse-proxy route + signed-cookie auth
steps.description: Add `src/server/preview-proxy.ts`: a request preamble that runs before the route table in `src/server/server.ts`. When the request `Host:` matches `run-<id>.<WARREN_PREVIEW_HOST>` (env-configured, e.g. `run-<id>.previews.example.com`), look up the run by id, resolve `preview_port` + `worker_id`, and forward the request (incl. WS upgrade) to that worker's loopback `127.0.0.1:<port>` via `burrowClientPool.clientFor({workerId})`. Signed-cookie auth: a new `GET /runs/:id/preview/login?token=<bearer>&redirect=<path>` route on the warren host validates the bearer and sets a `preview-<id>` HMAC-signed cookie (signed by `WARREN_API_TOKEN` or a derived key) scoped to the preview subdomain, then 302s to the redirect. The proxy preamble rejects requests without a valid cookie (or expired ≥ TTL). Tests cover: host-match dispatch, missing/expired cookie → 401, valid cookie → upstream forward, run not in `live` state → 503 with `preview_state` body. Cross-host routing (R-12) explicitly out of scope: assert `workerId === local worker name` and return 501 otherwise with a clear deferral message.

steps.title: Manual teardown route + audit event
steps.description: Add `POST /runs/:id/preview/teardown` (bearer-required) to `src/server/handlers.ts` + register in `src/server/router.ts`. Looks up the run; if `preview_state IN ('starting','live')`, calls the burrow kill-sidecar route, releases the port, sets `preview_state='torn-down'`, emits a `preview_torn_down` event (kind: control-plane operation, mx-4cf405 pattern). Idempotent: calling on a row already torn-down or never-started returns 200 with a no-op envelope. Tests: state-machine guards, idempotency, audit-event emission.

steps.title: UI — preview status badge, URL, teardown affordance
steps.description: On `src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx`: when `run.preview_state !== null`, render a `<PreviewPanel>` block above the event tail. Live state renders the URL as a clickable external link (target=_blank) with a green badge. Starting renders a spinner + 'Booting preview…'. Failed renders the `preview_failure_message` (last 200 lines, monospace, truncated with show-more). Torn-down renders a muted 'Preview ended' line with the teardown timestamp. A 'Tear down' button is visible in starting/live states; clicking calls `POST /runs/:id/preview/teardown` and optimistically transitions the badge. Update `src/ui/src/api/types.ts` with the new run fields. UI tests: each of the four states renders the expected shape.

steps.title: Acceptance scenario — 19-preview.ts (happy path + eviction path)
steps.description: `scripts/acceptance/scenarios/19-preview.ts`. Scenario seeds a project whose `.warren/defaults.json` declares a tiny static HTTP preview (`bun -e 'Bun.serve({port:3000, fetch:()=>new Response("ok")})'` or `python3 -m http.server 3000`). Variant (a): spawn a run with the stub agent + a prompt that exits cleanly so reap fires success; assert `preview_state` transitions starting → live (poll the warren run row); GET `https://run-<id>.<host>/` via the proxy with the signed cookie returns 200 body 'ok'; POST manual teardown transitions to torn-down. Variant (b): set `WARREN_PREVIEW_DEFAULT_TTL=2s`, sleep 3s after live, assert eviction sweep transitions to torn-down with reason=`ttl` on the audit event. Skip on `darwin` (bwrap-only feature; mx-1d31f0 container-only pattern). Use the inproc launcher + ScenarioCtx pool from mx-9296c9 / mx-eae655.

steps.title: Docs — operator setup + ROADMAP/SPEC update + record decisions
steps.description: (a) Update README.md + DEPLOY.md with the wildcard-CNAME + Caddy wildcard-cert ritual (Caddy snippet pointing at `:8080` with `*.<host>` and DNS-01 via Cloudflare/Route53, called out as honest config burden). (b) Document the new env vars: `WARREN_PREVIEW_HOST`, `WARREN_PREVIEW_PORT_RANGE`, `WARREN_PREVIEW_MAX_LIVE`, `WARREN_PREVIEW_DEFAULT_TTL`, `WARREN_PREVIEW_SWEEP_INTERVAL_MS`, signed-cookie secret derivation. (c) Mark ROADMAP R-19 status [in-progress] → [shipped] on merge; cross-reference seed warren-1bcb. (d) Confirm SPEC §11.L is in sync with the implementation. (e) `ml learn` then `ml record` for: same-sandbox default tradeoff, signed-cookie auth choice, port-allocator-in-runs-table decision, R-12 cross-host deferral, eviction-policy default ceiling.

risks: Cross-repo coordination drag: the burrow inbound-networking change (step 2) is on the critical path for step 6 and lives in a different repo with its own release cadence. If burrow can't ship within the warren milestone, steps 6–12 stall. Mitigation: file the burrow seed before step 3 starts so burrow work can run in parallel with warren-side scaffolding; pin `@os-eco/burrow-cli` in lockstep (CLAUDE.md three-places rule) so warren only consumes a known-good burrow.

Auth boundary on the preview subdomain: a misconfigured signed-cookie scope or a missing HMAC verification on the proxy preamble could turn a private-code preview into a public one. Mitigation: write a dedicated test that asserts requests without a valid cookie 401 (not 200 / not 502), and add a doctor check that warns when `WARREN_PREVIEW_HOST` is set but `WARREN_API_TOKEN` is the default placeholder.

Port exhaustion under load: a busy warren that runs out of the default 30000-31000 range silently fails new previews. Mitigation: emit a `preview_failed` event with reason=`port_exhausted` so it shows in the run event log; document the range expansion path; surface a doctor warning when ≥80% of the range is in use.

Same-sandbox side effects: the agent could leave the workspace in a state that breaks `preview.command` (deleted files, busy port, half-applied migrations). The preview will surface as `failed` per the ROADMAP UX, but operators will read it as 'warren is broken'. Mitigation: clearly surface `preview_failure_message` in the UI with the stderr tail so the failure mode is attributable to the agent's output, not warren.

Container memory pressure from idle `bun run dev` sidecars: per-run TTL alone is not enough — without the LRU cap, twenty long-TTL previews on a 1GB container OOMs the warren process. Mitigation: ship the LRU cap (step 7) in the same milestone as the per-TTL eviction; default `WARREN_PREVIEW_MAX_LIVE=20` and document the knob; add a `/readyz` warning when live preview count > 80% of cap.

Remote-worker future (R-12) interaction: deferring cross-host routing means the proxy preamble must return a clear 501 when `runs.worker_id` is not the local worker — silent fall-through to a closed loopback port would manifest as 'preview works for some runs, not others'. Mitigation: assert local-worker-only at the proxy preamble with an explicit error message naming R-12 as the deferral; acceptance scenario covers the assertion.

DNS-01 wildcard cert is operator burden the README has to be honest about: not every DNS provider Caddy supports is free, and the operator may not own DNS for `<warren-host>`. Mitigation: docs call out the constraint explicitly; alternative deployment recipe shows a per-project subdomain pattern operators control.
acceptance: SPEC §11.L exists and closes ROADMAP R-19's open questions; the burrow-side coordination seed in `../burrow/.seeds/` is filed and references warren-1bcb.
`.warren/defaults.json` accepts a `preview` block with `{enabled, command, port, readiness_path?, ttl?}`; missing-is-not-error; malformed surfaces in the per-file errors envelope (mx-66d478).
`runs` table has the five new preview columns; migration 0009 lands; `RunsRepo.attachPreview` follows the partial-input semantics of `attachStats` (mx-49272e).
Port allocator survives a warren restart: in-use ports are derived from `runs.preview_state IN ('starting','live')`, not in-memory state.

Reap's 5th best-effort sub-step launches a preview when `outcome==='succeeded'` and the project opted in; failure surfaces as `preview_state='failed'` with `preview_failure_message` populated and never fails the run.
Eviction sweep enforces both per-preview TTL and a global `WARREN_PREVIEW_MAX_LIVE` cap; both triggers emit `preview_evicted` events with `reason` set.

Host proxy preamble routes `Host: run-<id>.<host>` to `127.0.0.1:<preview_port>` for the run's owning worker; signed-cookie auth rejects unauthenticated browser requests; cross-host (`worker_id !== local`) returns 501 with a clear R-12 deferral message.
`POST /runs/:id/preview/teardown` is idempotent, bearer-required, emits a `preview_torn_down` audit event, and releases the port.
`RunDetail` UI renders preview state for all four states (starting / live / failed / torn-down) and exposes a teardown button in starting/live.
Acceptance scenario 19 passes both variants (happy path + TTL eviction) against a live warren+burrow stack on Linux; macOS skip is documented (mx-1d31f0 pattern).
Operator docs ship: README + DEPLOY explain wildcard CNAME + Caddy wildcard cert (Caddy snippet + DNS-01 provider list); all new env vars are documented in `.env.example`.

ROADMAP R-19 status moves [proposed] → [shipped] on merge; mulch records cover the same-sandbox tradeoff, signed-cookie auth choice, port-allocator-in-runs-table decision, R-12 deferral, and eviction-policy defaults.
`bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` all pass.

sections.context:
Warren's storage layer is SQLite-only today (SPEC §6, §3.2 lists 'no bring-your-own database' as a V1 non-goal). ROADMAP R-13 lifts that non-goal so SREs at 50+ engineer orgs can attach warren to their managed Postgres instead of operating state-in-a-docker-volume as the source of truth for what their engineers ran when. The realistic shape is dual-backend, not Postgres-only: SQLite stays the zero-config default for the standalone fresh-install path (CLAUDE.md 'GitHub URL + Anthropic key' promise), and Postgres lights up behind a WARREN_DB_URL env var.

Three code-level facts make this bigger than a config flip. (1) Schema (src/db/schema.ts:15) imports from drizzle-orm/sqlite-core; Drizzle has no shared sqlite/pg table builder — pg-core's pgTable is a different module with different column functions. (2) The client (src/db/client.ts:17-18) opens via drizzle-orm/bun-sqlite, a SYNCHRONOUS driver — every repo method calls .run() / .get() / .all() / .transaction(tx => ...) without await. drizzle-orm/node-postgres is async-first; every call returns a Promise. ~51 sync call sites across the 7 repos (agents, projects, runs, events, triggers, workers, burrows) and ~30 test files need to become async before Postgres can plug in. (3) The 8 migrations under src/db/migrations/ use SQLite-specific SQL — migration 0003_orphan_runs_on_project_delete.sql uses the canonical 12-step ALTER pattern (CREATE __new / INSERT SELECT / DROP / RENAME) with PRAGMA foreign_keys toggling that can't run on Postgres at all.

Burrow's per-worker SQLite stays untouched — that's run-local sandbox state, not org truth, and the warren↔burrow boundary is already clean (warren talks to burrow over HTTP-over-unix-socket, never reaches into burrow's DB). Only warren's own runs / events / projects / agents / triggers / workers / burrows tables move to Postgres-or-SQLite.
sections.approach:
Land R-13 as a sequenced refactor that keeps SQLite operators on the well-tested bun:sqlite path while adding Postgres behind a single env-var contract. Five seams change; everything else stays put.

(1) Repo layer goes async-first as a pure refactor (step 1). Every method on the 7 repos returns a Promise; the SQLite implementation wraps bun-sqlite's sync .run/.get/.all in Promise.resolve so behavior is unchanged. ~51 consumer call sites + tests pick up await keywords. This lands as one PR with the SQLite backend still active so any regression is bisectable against a pure-mechanical change.

(2) Schema splits into a shared metadata module + parallel dialect files (step 2). ROADMAP's 'Drizzle Kit emit both dialects from one schema file' sketch is approximate — drizzle doesn't actually support that. Instead, `src/db/schema/columns.ts` holds dialect-agnostic constants (enum tuples, column-name strings, type unions), and `src/db/schema/sqlite.ts` + `src/db/schema/postgres.ts` declare the two physical tables. JSON columns (3 today: agents.rendered_json, runs.rendered_agent_json, events.payload_json) map to `mode: "json"` on TEXT in SQLite and `jsonb` in pg. Text-enums (RUN_STATES, RUN_FAILURE_REASONS, EVENT_STREAMS, WORKER_STATES) stay TEXT in both (mx-2ab984: TS-only narrowing, no SQL CHECK) — pg native enums add migration churn for a TS-only check.

(3) Driver layer adds `drizzle-orm/node-postgres` and a dialect-aware openDatabase({ url }) (step 3). Parse: `sqlite:` / `file:` / bare path → bun-sqlite Database (today's path); `postgres:` / `postgresql:` → pg.Pool with `WARREN_DB_POOL_MAX` knob (default 10). WARREN_DB_PATH stays as a back-compat alias that synthesizes a sqlite:// URL.

(4) Postgres migrations regenerate from scratch via `drizzle-kit generate` against the pg schema, emitting to `src/db/migrations/postgres/` (step 4). The 12-step ALTER pattern in 0003 becomes a one-line pg ALTER TABLE; PRAGMA toggling disappears entirely. drizzle.config.ts becomes a pair (sqlite + postgres configs) or accepts a dialect flag.

(5) Boot + CLI + diagnostics + tests + acceptance + migration tool + docs land in the remaining steps. The test substrate is the riskiest call — testcontainers vs pglite vs CI-gated env-var; step 6 commits to one. The one-shot `warren db migrate-to-postgres --from <sqlite> --to <pg-url>` (step 8) is the upgrade path for existing operators.

alternatives.name: Postgres-only V2 (drop SQLite entirely)
alternatives.rejected_because: Kills the standalone fresh-install story the CLAUDE.md opener promises: 'a user with a GitHub URL and an Anthropic key can dispatch a run end-to-end with no other tooling.' SQLite remains the right default for the home-server appliance posture (§3.1) and the demo path. ROADMAP R-13 explicitly frames this as 'bring-your-own', not 'replace SQLite'.

alternatives.name: Switch SQLite driver to drizzle-orm/libsql for native async on both dialects
alternatives.rejected_because: libsql is async, which would make the async-repo refactor cleaner — but it also changes the SQLite path's runtime semantics for every existing operator. The Promise.resolve-wrapped bun-sqlite path keeps the today-working driver in place; the async wrapper is mechanical and reversible. Re-evaluate libsql as a follow-up if the wrapper proves awkward; it's not a one-way door.

alternatives.name: MySQL alongside Postgres in this seed
alternatives.rejected_because: ROADMAP R-13 explicitly defers MySQL ('Postgres only for V2; MySQL as a follow-up if a real consumer asks'). The dialect-abstraction work in steps 2-4 keeps MySQL a config change, not a code change.

alternatives.name: ORM swap to Kysely or Prisma for richer dialect abstraction
alternatives.rejected_because: Drizzle is the os-eco-wide convention (mirrors burrow + seeds; mx-27f236). The migration filename pinning pattern (mx-b4f9b5) and the drizzle generate workflow (mx-3f03bd) are already locked in. A Drizzle-to-X swap is a much bigger surface change than R-13 needs.

alternatives.name: Two fully-independent schema files (no shared columns module)
alternatives.rejected_because: Drift risk: column names, FK semantics, indexes, and enum tuples must stay byte-identical so a row written by one dialect can be read by the other (the migration tool in step 8 depends on this). A shared `columns.ts` constants module + thin dialect adapters is small enough that the shared layer earns its keep.

alternatives.name: Auto-detect SQLite vs Postgres from WARREN_DB_PATH only (no new env var)
alternatives.rejected_because: WARREN_DB_PATH is path-shaped; a postgres://... value in a `_PATH` variable is operator-hostile and breaks all the docs assuming filesystem semantics. WARREN_DB_URL is the standard env-var shape across the ecosystem (PostgreSQL's PGDATABASE_URL, Rails' DATABASE_URL); WARREN_DB_PATH stays as a back-compat alias for SQLite operators.

steps.title: Async repo layer: convert all 7 repos + ~51 consumers to Promise-returning methods
steps.description: Make every method on AgentsRepo, ProjectsRepo, RunsRepo, EventsRepo, TriggersRepo, WorkersRepo, BurrowsRepo return Promise<T>. Wrap bun-sqlite's sync .run() / .get() / .all() in Promise.resolve at the implementation site. Convert .transaction((tx) => {...}) bodies to async ((tx) => {...}) — the bun-sqlite transaction callback is sync today but node-postgres requires async, so the warren-side seam adopts the pg-compatible signature on both backends. Audit + add await at ~51 call sites across src/server, src/runs, src/projects, src/cli, src/triggers, src/supervisor, src/diagnostics. Update ~30 test files. WarrenDb.close() also becomes async (pg.Pool.end() is async; bun-sqlite's close stays sync but wraps cleanly). Pure refactor — no schema change, no new dependencies, SQLite remains the only backend after this lands. All existing tests pass unmodified except for await keywords. Lands as one PR so a regression is bisectable against a mechanical change.

steps.title: Schema dialect split: shared columns module + parallel sqlite/postgres tables
steps.description: Refactor src/db/schema.ts into: (a) src/db/schema/columns.ts — dialect-agnostic constants (RUN_STATES, RUN_FAILURE_REASONS, EVENT_STREAMS, WORKER_STATES, column-name strings, type unions, the makeTriggerRowId helper); (b) src/db/schema/sqlite.ts — today's sqliteTable definitions, imported from columns.ts; (c) src/db/schema/postgres.ts — mirror pgTable definitions. Map JSON columns (agents.rendered_json, runs.rendered_agent_json, events.payload_json) to `mode:"json"` on TEXT for SQLite, `jsonb` for pg. Text-enums stay TEXT on both (mx-2ab984). Timestamps stay TEXT ISO8601 on both (mx-a6e5be — burrow uses integer mode; warren deliberately diverges and that diverge should stay symmetric across dialects). `runs.cost_usd` stays `real` on both — pg's float4 is acceptable for the best-effort accounting (warren-a7dc). `Repos` becomes generic over the dialect's table set; the columns module exports the row/insert types both dialects share. Add a CI lint script that asserts the two schema files declare identical column lists, indexes, and FK targets — drift is the failure mode.

steps.title: Postgres driver + dialect-aware openDatabase + WARREN_DB_URL env contract
steps.description: Add `pg` + `drizzle-orm/node-postgres` deps to package.json. Replace `openDatabase({ path })` with `openDatabase({ url })` in src/db/client.ts. Parse rules: bare path or `sqlite:` / `file:` URL → bun-sqlite Database (today's path, mkdir + WAL + PRAGMA busy_timeout); `postgres:` / `postgresql:` URL → pg.Pool({ connectionString, max: WARREN_DB_POOL_MAX ?? 10 }). Both dialects return the same WarrenDb shape (.drizzle, .close() — drop .raw, or expose it via a dialect-tagged union so callers that need raw access narrow first). Honor `WARREN_DB_URL` first; `WARREN_DB_PATH` synthesizes a `sqlite://<path>` URL for back-compat (src/server/config.ts:57 + src/cli/context.ts:32). Document `WARREN_DB_POOL_MAX` (pg only) and the PgBouncer recommendation in code comments. ServerConfig grows a `dbUrl: string` field; `dbPath` becomes a derived getter that errors on pg URLs.

steps.title: Postgres migration set: regenerate 0000-0008 against pg schema; per-dialect drizzle.config
steps.description: Convert `drizzle.config.ts` to a per-dialect pair: `drizzle.config.sqlite.ts` (today's content) + `drizzle.config.postgres.ts` (dialect: 'postgresql', schema: './src/db/schema/postgres.ts', out: './src/db/migrations/postgres'). Add `bun run db:generate:sqlite` + `bun run db:generate:postgres` scripts. Regenerate the full migration set from scratch against the pg schema — migrations land under `src/db/migrations/postgres/` with their own journal. Verify: (a) JSON columns emit as `jsonb`; (b) the 12-step ALTER in 0003 collapses to a one-line `ALTER TABLE runs ALTER COLUMN project_id DROP NOT NULL` + FK rebuild; (c) PRAGMA toggling disappears entirely from the pg path; (d) `runs_project_started_idx` and `runs_agent_started_idx` (DESC expressions) emit cleanly without backtick quoting (mx-451cd7 risk pattern but pg-shaped). Apply the rename + journal pin convention (mx-3f03bd, mx-b4f9b5, mx-e078d8) to each generated migration. The two folders evolve in lockstep — adding a new SQLite migration without its pg twin is a CI-blocking lint failure.

steps.title: Boot wiring + diagnostics + CLI: read WARREN_DB_URL across server, CLI, doctor
steps.description: Update `bootServer` (src/server/main.ts:78) to call `openDatabase({ url: serverConfig.dbUrl })`. Update `withCliDb` (src/cli/context.ts:46) the same way. Add a `db_reachable` diagnostic check to src/diagnostics/checks.ts that runs `SELECT 1` against the active backend (with a short timeout) and reports the dialect in its message — surfaced in `warren doctor` and `/readyz` alongside the existing 8 checks (mx-1a70ef). The check fails loudly with a recovery hint pointing at WARREN_DB_URL. Update `src/server/config.ts`'s top-of-file env contract docstring to describe both backends. Bump `WarrenDb.close()` callers to await (already groundwork in step 1; this step verifies the supervisor's SIGTERM path also awaits the pool close on the pg backend — graceful shutdown contract).

steps.title: Test infrastructure: dialect-aware withDb() helper + pg substrate decision
steps.description: Today's tests use `openDatabase({ path: ':memory:' })` at ~30 sites (mostly src/**/*.test.ts). Add a `withDb(dialect?: 'sqlite' | 'postgres')` test helper that produces a fresh DB per test, isolated per dialect. Substrate decision (lock in this step): (a) testcontainers + a shared per-suite pg container, or (b) pglite (embedded WASM postgres, single-binary), or (c) `WARREN_TEST_PG_URL` env-var gate so CI brings up its own pg service and dev opts in. Recommendation: pglite for unit tests (cheap, no daemon, runs in CI on every push) + testcontainers for the new acceptance scenario in step 7. Run the existing test suite against both backends. The `:memory:` test pattern stays for the SQLite path. New CI matrix entry: `WARREN_TEST_DIALECT={sqlite,postgres} bun test`.

steps.title: Acceptance scenario 19: warren-on-postgres end-to-end
steps.description: Add scripts/acceptance/scenarios/19-postgres-backend.ts. Boot warren+burrow against a Postgres container (testcontainers, picking up the substrate from step 6); register an agent; add a project; dispatch a run; stream events; finalize. Restart warren mid-stream; verify event resume from MAX(events.burrow_event_seq) + 1 works the same on pg as on SQLite (SPEC §9). Verify run history survives the restart. Add an `acceptance:pg` script entry (or auto-detect via WARREN_DB_URL scheme). Cleanup is mandatory — the scenario tears down its pg container even on test failure, matching the deterministic + idempotent + self-cleaning convention for acceptance scenarios.

steps.title: `warren db migrate-to-postgres` one-shot tool
steps.description: Add src/cli/commands/db-migrate-to-postgres.ts: `warren db migrate-to-postgres --from <sqlite-path> --to <pg-url>`. Opens both backends, runs pg migrations to head, table-by-table COPY with FK ordering (agents → projects → runs → events → triggers → workers → burrows). Idempotent: skips rows already present by primary key, prints `n new / n skipped / n total` per table, fails loud on PK collisions where row contents differ. Streams events in batches (events.payload_json is the heavy column; 1k-row batches keep memory bounded). Verifies row counts match per table after copy and exits non-zero with a per-table diff on mismatch. Documented in README's deploy section as the upgrade path for existing SQLite operators. No in-place online migration — operator pauses warren, runs the tool, points WARREN_DB_URL at pg, restarts.

steps.title: Docs: SPEC §3.2/§6/§10, README, .env.example, fly.toml, docker-compose, ROADMAP
steps.description: Remove 'no bring-your-own database' from SPEC §3.2 V1 non-goals (it's no longer one). Update SPEC §6 (DB row in Tech Stack table) and §11 (decisions) with the dual-backend story + WARREN_DB_URL contract. Add a SPEC §10 subsection covering the Postgres deploy path alongside today's home-server + Fly.io stories. README: introduce 'Standalone (SQLite, default)' and 'Org-scale (Postgres)' subsections at the top of deploy/config docs — the standalone path stays primary per CLAUDE.md positioning, with Postgres framed as a feature that lights up when WARREN_DB_URL is set. Add WARREN_DB_URL + WARREN_DB_POOL_MAX entries to .env.example (mx-9c878d convention). Add an opt-in pg-backed example to docker-compose.yml comments. fly.toml: comment that the volume mount is unnecessary if attaching to a managed pg. Flip ROADMAP R-13 status to `[shipped]` with the dated decision record (matching R-02's shipped-shape narrative). Record `ml` decisions in db + build + server domains capturing the schema-split rule, the WARREN_DB_URL contract, the per-dialect drizzle.config pair, and the async-repo refactor pattern.

risks: Sync→async repo refactor blast radius: ~51 call sites + ~30 test files pick up await keywords in step 1. Drizzle's transaction callback signature differs between bun-sqlite (sync) and node-postgres (async); the warren-side seam adopts the async-callback signature on both backends to keep the API uniform. Mitigation: land step 1 as a pure refactor with SQLite still as the only backend and zero new dependencies, so a regression is bisectable against a mechanical change. Run the full test + lint + typecheck trinity after each repo's conversion.

Schema dialect drift between src/db/schema/sqlite.ts and src/db/schema/postgres.ts. Column names, FK targets, indexes, enum tuples, and nullability must stay byte-identical or the step-8 migration tool corrupts data. Mitigation: shared src/db/schema/columns.ts module + a CI lint script that diffs the two schema files' shape (column lists, types-mod-dialect, index names + columns, FK targets) and fails on drift. Test the migration tool round-trips against both dialects.

Migration set divergence: each schema change must emit BOTH sqlite and postgres migrations in lockstep (mx-fae553 lockstep pattern, sibling to triggers/warren-config). Mitigation: a `bun run db:generate` script in package.json that emits to both folders + a CI gate that fails when only one folder has a new migration. Document the workflow in CLAUDE.md alongside the existing drizzle migration convention (mx-3f03bd).

Existing migration 0003_orphan_runs_on_project_delete.sql uses the 12-step SQLite ALTER pattern with PRAGMA foreign_keys toggling (mx-9c90e8, mx-f45af5). None of that exists in Postgres. Mitigation: regenerate the full pg migration set from scratch against the pg schema (step 4); don't translate the SQLite migrations one-to-one. The journal entry is the migration's identity — pg migrations get their own journal under src/db/migrations/postgres/meta/_journal.json.

Postgres connection pool starvation under load. Default Pool({ max: 10 }) can wedge if warren's long-lived event-stream connections + a burst of run-dispatch + the scheduler tick all reach for sockets at once. Mitigation: WARREN_DB_POOL_MAX env knob; document PgBouncer (transaction-pool mode) as the recommended production path. Step 7's acceptance scenario should exercise concurrent run dispatch with WARREN_DB_POOL_MAX=2 to surface the failure mode.

Graceful shutdown semantics differ: bun-sqlite's db.close() is sync; pg.Pool.end() is async. The supervisor's SIGTERM/SIGINT handler must await the close on the pg backend or risk dropping mid-flight queries. Mitigation: WarrenDb.close() becomes async in step 1; supervisor/main.ts already awaits subordinate shutdowns. Test by SIGTERM-ing warren during a streaming run and verifying clean exit.

Test infrastructure for the pg path adds CI cost and flakiness. testcontainers spawns a Docker daemon; pglite needs a WASM runtime; the env-var-gated variant skips coverage in dev. Mitigation: pglite for unit tests (lightweight, no daemon, runs everywhere), testcontainers only for acceptance scenarios (one container per scenario, torn down on exit). Document opt-out via WARREN_TEST_DIALECT=sqlite for dev.

Operator confusion between WARREN_DB_PATH and WARREN_DB_URL. Today's docs and acceptance scripts hardcode WARREN_DB_PATH. Mitigation: WARREN_DB_PATH stays as a back-compat alias that synthesizes sqlite://<path>; warn in /readyz or doctor output if both are set and they disagree; update acceptance/lib/inproc.ts and the doctor/init scenarios to prefer WARREN_DB_URL going forward.

Built-in agent seeding (src/server/main.ts seedBuiltinAgents) and the SQLite `:memory:` test pattern (mx-d5fe85 layout) both assume sync openDatabase. The step-1 async refactor surfaces these; step 5 (boot wiring) verifies they re-converge against an async openDatabase. Track seedBuiltinAgents as a hot path in CI — first-boot regressions there are user-facing.
acceptance: Zero-config back-compat: a warren deploy with no WARREN_DB_URL and no WARREN_DB_PATH (or WARREN_DB_URL=sqlite:///data/warren.db) behaves identically to today — same migrations apply, same schema, same WAL pragmas, all existing tests pass unmodified except for await keywords picked up in step 1.

WARREN_DB_URL=postgres://user:pass@host/db boots warren successfully; pg migrations apply on first start; SELECT 1 probe passes in `warren doctor` and `/readyz` with a `db_reachable` check tagged with the active dialect.

Full test suite passes against both backends in CI: WARREN_TEST_DIALECT=sqlite (default, today's :memory: path) AND WARREN_TEST_DIALECT=postgres (pglite or testcontainers per step 6). `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` clean on both.

Acceptance scenario 19 (warren-on-postgres) dispatches a run end-to-end against a Postgres container, streams events, restarts warren mid-stream, and verifies event resume from MAX(events.burrow_event_seq)+1 works the same as on SQLite (SPEC §9 restart-recovery contract).

`warren db migrate-to-postgres --from <sqlite> --to <pg-url>` copies a populated SQLite DB into Postgres: row counts match per table, PKs are preserved byte-for-byte, FK-ordered table walk completes without errors, re-running the tool is idempotent (skips existing rows by PK).

Schema drift lint passes: src/db/schema/sqlite.ts and src/db/schema/postgres.ts declare identical column lists, indexes, FK targets, and enum tuples; the CI gate fails any commit that adds a column to one but not the other.
Migration parity lint passes: every commit that adds a file under src/db/migrations/ also adds the matching file under src/db/migrations/postgres/ (or vice versa) with a matching journal tag.

SPEC §3.2 no longer lists `no bring-your-own database` as a V1 non-goal. SPEC §6 and §10 document the WARREN_DB_URL contract and the dual-backend deploy story. ROADMAP R-13 status flips to `[shipped]` with a dated decision record. README's deploy section surfaces SQLite (default) and Postgres (org-scale) paths with the standalone framing preserved.

`ml` decisions recorded in db + build + server domains covering: schema-split rule, WARREN_DB_URL env contract, per-dialect drizzle.config pair, async-repo refactor pattern, migration-parity CI gate, and pg test substrate choice.

sections.context: R-13 (Postgres backend) shipped half-complete in v0.3.2. Outer wiring (URL parsing, openDatabase, migrations, schema parity via drift.test.ts) is real, but the 7 repos in src/db/repos/*.ts use sqlite-only drizzle APIs internally: 15 .get() calls, 23 .run() calls, 14 .all() calls, and 4 sync db.transaction() calls. Two dialect guards (src/server/main.ts:createReposForDialect and src/cli/context.ts:withCliDb) backstop this with a fail-fast error. WARREN_DB_URL=postgres:// boots, runs migrations against pg cleanly, then crashes the moment any repo method is invoked. Discovery happened during the 2026-05-14 dogfood deploy to Fly with Supabase — see ml record build/postgres-half-shipped-r13. Scenario 19 (warren-on-postgres) was skip-gated on WARREN_TEST_PG_URL which CI never sets, so the gap was never caught at merge time. README and fly.toml advertise Supabase as a supported WARREN_DB_URL target; that promise is currently false.
sections.approach: Introduce a thin dialect-polymorphic adapter in src/db/repos/drizzle-adapter.ts that maps sqlite's sync .get()/.run()/.all() and sync db.transaction() to dialect-agnostic async equivalents (pickOne/pickAll/runWrite/runInTransaction). Repos migrate to the adapter one at a time, smallest-first so risk is bounded. Once all 7 repos compile against AnyWarrenDb, widen createRepos's signature, delete the two guards, and wire a postgres service into CI so scenario 19 and unit-test matrix actually run on every PR. Finish with a real Supabase dogfood deploy to validate end-to-end. Chose the adapter over per-repo rewrites because it concentrates the dialect-handling code in one auditable file (~50 LOC) and keeps repo bodies readable.

alternatives.name: Duplicate each repo as Sqlite*Repo and Postgres*Repo, select at boot
alternatives.rejected_because: Doubles code and tests, diverges drift over time, violates the existing 'one repo per table' contract.

alternatives.name: Inline the dialect-agnostic pattern (.limit(1) + array index, await + .execute()) across all 56 call sites without an adapter
alternatives.rejected_because: Spreads the dialect-handling transformation across 56 call sites; harder to enforce uniformity and easy to regress.

alternatives.name: Drop Postgres support entirely, retract R-13 in CHANGELOG
alternatives.rejected_because: Postgres is the cloud-scale story; SPEC §3.2 and the operator deploy targets (fly.io, Supabase, RDS) commit to it.

steps.title: Add dialect-polymorphic drizzle adapter (src/db/repos/drizzle-adapter.ts)
steps.description: New module exports pickOne<T>(qb): Promise<T|undefined>, pickAll<T>(qb): Promise<T[]>, runWrite(qb): Promise<void>, and runInTransaction(db, async fn): Promise<T>. Each helper accepts either dialect's drizzle handle and uses the async-compatible pattern (.limit(1) + array index for pickOne, await for pickAll, await + .execute() for runWrite, async transaction callback). Add unit tests against both sqlite (:memory:) and pg (env-gated on WARREN_TEST_PG_URL).

steps.title: Migrate AgentsRepo + BurrowsRepo to adapter (smallest pair)
steps.description: AgentsRepo (2 get + 3 run + 1 all + 1 transaction) and BurrowsRepo (1 get + 2 run + 2 all + 0 transaction). Repos accept AnyDrizzleDb instead of DrizzleDb (sqlite alias). Update their .test.ts files to run under withDb() in both dialects when WARREN_TEST_PG_URL is set. Acceptance: existing sqlite tests pass, pg tests pass when env set.

steps.title: Migrate ProjectsRepo + EventsRepo to adapter
steps.description: ProjectsRepo (2 get + 3 run + 1 all + 0 transaction) and EventsRepo (3 get + 0 run + 2 all + 0 transaction). Same widening pattern. Events is the high-throughput path so verify pg performance with the bridge stream test.

steps.title: Migrate TriggersRepo + WorkersRepo to adapter
steps.description: TriggersRepo (2 get + 3 run + 2 all + 1 transaction) and WorkersRepo (2 get + 4 run + 1 all + 1 transaction). Same widening pattern.

steps.title: Migrate RunsRepo to adapter (largest, has assertRunTransition coupling)
steps.description: RunsRepo (3 get + 8 run + 5 all + 1 transaction) — the largest. Mind the assertRunTransition state-machine logic. Verify run lifecycle (create → in_progress → reaped/cancelled/failed) works on pg.

steps.title: Add WARREN_TEST_PG_URL postgres service to CI (warren-fe03 fold-in)
steps.description: Update .github/workflows/ci.yml to spin up a postgres:16 service, set WARREN_TEST_PG_URL=postgres://warren:warren@127.0.0.1:5432/warren_test on a matrix job, and run bun test in both sqlite and pg modes. Required so the widened repo tests from steps 2-5 actually execute on every PR. Closes warren-fe03.

steps.title: Widen createRepos to AnyWarrenDb; remove dialect guards
steps.description: src/db/repos/index.ts: createRepos accepts AnyWarrenDb. Delete src/server/main.ts:createReposForDialect (use createRepos directly at line 98). Delete the dialect check in src/cli/context.ts:withCliDb. Run typecheck + lint + the full bun test suite (now exercised in both dialects via step 6).

steps.title: Run acceptance scenario 19 against real postgres; verify green
steps.description: WARREN_TEST_PG_URL=... bun run scripts/acceptance/scenarios/19-warren-on-postgres.ts. The scenario already exists and tests boot + run dispatch + kill-mid-stream + restart-recovery on pg. Fix any surfacing bugs. Add scenario to the matrix CI job from step 6.

steps.title: CHANGELOG correction (warren-5549 fold-in)
steps.description: Amend the v0.3.2 entry to clarify R-13 was provisioning-only at that release; add the current entry that records the actual end-to-end postgres ship. Update README's 'org-scale path' callout. Closes warren-5549.

steps.title: Re-attempt dogfood deploy on Fly + Supabase; verify end-to-end
steps.description: Restore WARREN_DB_URL secret on warren-deployed (Supabase project warren-dogfood still exists at biaxgkadzaruhrqsjnmi). Use the session-pooler URL with uselibpqcompat=true to bypass the pg-connection-string SSL verify-full chain issue. Start the machine, verify /healthz green, verify drizzle migrations applied to Supabase, dispatch a test run from the UI. Document in deploy notes / README.

risks: Drizzle's async transaction signature differs between sqlite and pg in subtle ways (rollback semantics, isolation defaults). Mitigation: adapter's runInTransaction normalizes; unit-test both branches.

Postgres connection pool exhaustion under bridge fan-out — pg-pool default max is 10. Mitigation: WARREN_DB_POOL_MAX env var already exists (server/main.ts:resolvePgPoolMax); ensure it's documented in .env.example and surfaced in doctor.

Scenario 19 may surface restart-recovery edge cases (events resume from MAX(seq)+1) that work on sqlite but not on pg due to different lock semantics. Mitigation: scenario 19 is the validation gate — if it goes green, the SPEC §9 contract holds.

Some sqlite-specific bookkeeping (preview port allocator, R-19) is intentionally sqlite-only per the existing 'dialect=sqlite' branches in handlers.ts and doctor.ts. Don't widen those; document the gap as a follow-up if pg preview support is wanted.
acceptance: WARREN_DB_URL=postgres://... boots warren cleanly, runs migrations, serves /healthz=200 and /readyz with db_reachable.dialect=postgres.
All unit tests pass in both WARREN_TEST_DIALECT=sqlite and WARREN_TEST_DIALECT=postgres modes; CI runs both.
Acceptance scenario 19 (warren-on-postgres) passes end-to-end against a real postgres (not a stub).
Dialect guards (createReposForDialect in main.ts; withCliDb check in cli/context.ts) are removed.
CHANGELOG accurately reflects when end-to-end postgres support actually shipped (warren-5549 resolved).
Dogfood deploy on Fly + Supabase succeeds: warren-deployed.fly.dev serves traffic, migrations applied to Supabase, a test run completes end-to-end.

sections.context: R-19 previews (§11.L) require operators to own a domain, configure a wildcard CNAME, and provision a wildcard TLS cert via DNS-01 — a closed door for the common self-hoster who just `fly deploy`s warren. Path-based preview mode lets warren reuse the single hostname+cert already serving the UI, removing the only deploy-time prerequisite that depended on external DNS/cert infrastructure. This unblocks the standalone quickstart story and removes the domain-purchase blocker on warren-724e (dogfood).
sections.approach: Add a `WARREN_PREVIEW_MODE=path|subdomain` env var (default `path`). In path mode previews are served at `https://<warren-host>/p/<run-id>/...` from the same Bun process that serves the API/UI; subdomain mode (today's behavior) stays opt-in for multi-tenant operators. The proxy preamble (`src/preview/proxy.ts`) gains a path-prefix match branch; the signed cookie scope (`src/preview/cookie.ts`) narrows from `Domain=.<host>` to `Path=/p/<run-id>/`; PR annotation (`src/runs/pr-annotate.ts`) emits the path URL when mode=path. HTML rewriting (best-effort `<base href>` injection on `text/html` responses + `Location:` header rewriting on 3xx) keeps root-relative asset URLs and dev-server redirects from escaping the prefix. JSON/JS/CSS pass through untouched. Chosen over a separate path-only feature flag because it cleanly extends the existing PreviewConfigSchema and proxy seam without forking the lifecycle/eviction code, which stays mode-agnostic.

alternatives.name: Subdomain-only (status quo)
alternatives.rejected_because: Closes the door for zero-domain operators — the motivating use case for this follow-up.

alternatives.name: Path mode without HTML rewriting
alternatives.rejected_because: SPAs with root-relative assets (/assets/foo.js) break under the /p/<id>/ prefix; <base href> injection is the cheapest workable shim.

alternatives.name: Per-run subdomain on a free shared host (e.g. fly.dev wildcard)
alternatives.rejected_because: Leaks run ids to the operator's cloud DNS and depends on the host platform's wildcard policy.

steps.title: SPEC §11.L addendum: path-based preview mode design lock
steps.description: Extend SPEC §11.L with a 'Routing modes' subsection: `WARREN_PREVIEW_MODE=path|subdomain` (default `path`), URL contract (`/p/<run-id>/...`), HTML rewrite contract (<base href> injection + Location: rewrite, text/html only, best-effort), cookie scope contract (Path= vs Domain= per mode), single-tenant-per-host caveat, and mode-gated doctor checks. Cross-link from §11.L 'Auth' and 'Routing' paragraphs; no code change.

steps.title: PreviewModeSchema + env loader + .warren/preview.yaml field
steps.description: Add `PreviewMode = 'path' | 'subdomain'` to `src/preview/launch.ts` (or `src/warren-config/schema.ts` per locality) with default `path`. Wire `WARREN_PREVIEW_MODE` into `loadPreviewLaunchConfigFromEnv`; thread mode through `PreviewLaunchConfig`. Update `mx-062a6d` (env loader) and `mx-cc4a4f` (PreviewConfigSchema) records. Tests cover default, explicit subdomain, invalid value rejection.

steps.title: Proxy preamble path-match branch
steps.description: In `src/preview/proxy.ts` (and its mount in `src/server/main.ts`), add a path-prefix branch: when mode=path, match `^/p/(?<runId>[a-z0-9-]+)(/.*)?$`, strip prefix, resolve `runs.preview_port → 127.0.0.1:<port>`, forward HTTP+WS via `burrowClientPool.clientFor`. Host-match branch stays for subdomain mode. `preview_last_hit_at` debounce (mx-411a6f) lifts unchanged. Cross-host (worker_id != local) still 501s with R-12 deferral message. Tests: path match, prefix strip, unknown runId → 404 (not 502), WS upgrade preserved.

steps.title: HTML rewriting middleware (<base> injection + Location: rewrite)
steps.description: Add a response transform in the path-mode proxy branch: (a) on `Content-Type: text/html` responses, inject `<base href="/p/<run-id>/">` immediately after the opening `<head>` tag using a streaming or buffered rewriter (Bun's HTMLRewriter or a minimal regex shim); skip if no <head> present. (b) On 3xx responses, if `Location:` starts with `/` and not `/p/<run-id>/`, rewrite to `/p/<run-id>/...`. JSON/JS/CSS/binary pass through untouched. Tests: basic HTML injection, no-head HTML (no-op), Location header rewrite (relative, absolute same-host, absolute external left alone), content-type negative case.

steps.title: Signed-cookie path-scope variant
steps.description: In `src/preview/cookie.ts`, parameterize cookie issuance by mode: path mode → `Path=/p/<run-id>/; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Lax` (no Domain); subdomain mode keeps `Domain=.<warren-host>; Path=/`. Add a path-mode login route variant: either keep `/runs/:id/preview/login` and redirect to `/p/<run-id>/` or add `GET /p/<run-id>/__warren/login?token=…`. Update `mx-c38965`. Tests: cookie scope per mode, HMAC verification still valid across both, unauthenticated path-mode request → 401.

steps.title: PR annotation URL shape branch
steps.description: In `src/runs/pr-annotate.ts` (mx-ba79c4), branch on mode: path → `https://<warren-host>/p/<run-id>/`, subdomain → `https://run-<id>.<warren-host>/`. `<!-- warren:preview-start --> / <!-- warren:preview-end -->` markers unchanged. The `preview_url_or_placeholder` fragment in `PR_FRAGMENT_NAMES` consumes the mode-aware URL. Tests: both URL shapes; idempotent re-annotate.

steps.title: UI: RunDetail preview URL + teardown affordance honor mode
steps.description: Update `src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx` PreviewCard (mx-67c922) and `buildPreviewLoginUrl` in `src/ui/src/api/client.ts` (mx-801a37) to construct path-mode URLs. The server hands the UI the assembled preview base URL (added to `RunDetail` response payload) so the UI stays mode-agnostic. Update `REFETCH_TRIGGER_KINDS` semantics unchanged (mx-6d9b99).

steps.title: Acceptance scenario variant: 20-preview-path.ts
steps.description: Clone `scripts/acceptance/scenarios/20-preview.ts` into a `20-preview-path.ts` (or parametrize the existing scenario with a `mode` variant) that boots warren with `WARREN_PREVIEW_MODE=path`, exercises the `/p/<run-id>/` URL shape end-to-end (happy-path + cookie-auth), and asserts <base href> injection on the served HTML. Reuse the python3 -m http.server fixture pattern (mx-87d34c). macOS still skipped per mx-1d31f0. Doctor check passes without WARREN_PREVIEW_HOST set.

risks: <base href> injection breaks apps that already set their own <base> — mitigation: skip injection when an existing <base> tag is present in <head>.
Apps that compute URLs from window.location.host assuming host == app may misbehave under /p/<id>/ — documented trade-off; operators with such apps can use subdomain mode.
Streaming HTML rewriter buffers full response if naive — bound the rewrite to first ~64KB of <head> or use Bun's HTMLRewriter for streaming.

Single-tenant-per-host caveat: path mode shares cookies across runs on the same host (mitigated by Path-scoped cookies, but reviewers logged into one preview can't simultaneously hold sessions for two on the same browser without per-path cookies — verify Path scope is honored).
acceptance: SPEC §11.L documents path mode as the default with the WARREN_PREVIEW_MODE env, URL contract, cookie scope contract, and HTML rewrite contract.
A fresh `fly deploy` of warren with no DNS/cert changes serves a working preview at https://<warren-host>/p/<run-id>/ end-to-end (acceptance scenario 20-preview-path green on Linux).
Subdomain mode still works unchanged (existing scenario 20-preview stays green).
PR annotation emits the path-mode URL when WARREN_PREVIEW_MODE=path and the subdomain URL otherwise.
Signed-cookie auth rejects unauthenticated requests in both modes (401, not 502).
bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck all green.

sections.context: Seeds v0.4.3 (2026-05-10) shipped the consumer-facing pieces of R-01 in seeds itself: Issue.extensions on the schema, `sd update --extensions <json>` with shallow-merge semantics, `--clear-extensions`, an Extensions line in `sd show`, and `sd ready --respect-schedule`. ROADMAP.md:46-62 plus the cross-repo dashboard (ROADMAP.md:1501) confirm the seeds half is green. Warren has consumed exactly one slice: `src/triggers/seeds-extension.ts` calls `sd update --extensions '{scheduledFor:null,lastScheduledRun:runId}'` after a cron fire. None of the other warren-owned namespaced keys from the convention (role, trigger, lastRunId, lastRunAt, queued) are produced or consumed anywhere. ROADMAP.md:1539-1542 spells out the remaining work: wire `sd update --extensions` calls into spawn (and reap, if we redefine lastRunAt) and read `extensions.{role,scheduledFor,trigger,lastRunId}` in the issues UI. The UI half folds into R-04 because no issues page exists yet; this plan closes the producer side cleanly so R-04 can light up reads later without re-litigating shape.
sections.approach: Generalize the existing triggers-only helper into a shared `src/seeds-cli/` facade that owns the typed warren-namespaced extension schema, then thread an optional `seedId` through SpawnRunInput + HTTP POST /runs + the runs row (new `seed_id` column) so spawnRun has the seed to write back to. After provision+dispatch lands, fire-and-log a single `updateExtensions(seedId, {role, trigger, lastRunId, lastRunAt})` merge — failures log a system event but never roll back the run, mirroring the established pl-2f15 risk-4 mitigation in triggers/tick.ts. Cron path collapses to a single merged write that combines today's `{scheduledFor:null, lastScheduledRun:runId}` with the new common keys. UI surface is intentionally tiny: expose `seedId` on the Run JSON shape and surface a back-link on RunDetail. Issues-page reads of `extensions.{role,trigger,scheduledFor,lastRunId}` ride R-04 — they need a page to land on first. This keeps R-01 closure scoped to the producer convention while leaving zero ambiguity for R-04 about the shape it should read.

alternatives.name: Keep helper inside src/triggers/
alternatives.rejected_because: The trigger flavor (clearScheduledFor) is one specialization of a more general producer-side API. Co-locating new manual/comment dispatch logic inside triggers/ confuses module ownership and forces non-trigger code to import from a triggers module.

alternatives.name: Skip seed_id on the runs row; re-derive from seeds on read
alternatives.rejected_because: Runs outlive a project clone refresh, and seeds extensions are mutable. Persisting the binding makes lastRunId in seeds authoritative and the warren-side seed_id an inverse index without per-read re-derivation. The DB column is nullable and cheap.

alternatives.name: Validate warren extension keys inside seeds (per-namespace JSON Schema)
alternatives.rejected_because: ROADMAP.md:107-108 explicitly leans leave-it-to-the-consumer: warren validates its own keys with zod inside warren so seeds stays schema-stable and other consumers (greenhouse, overstory) don't get blocked on a central registry.

alternatives.name: Block R-01 closure on R-04 (issues UI)
alternatives.rejected_because: Producer side is small (~a day), shape is settled, and shipping it now means R-04 inherits a working write-path instead of designing one mid-flight. Splitting also lets the cron tick path collapse from two sd updates to one without waiting on an unrelated UI item.

steps.title: Extract src/seeds-cli/ from src/triggers/seeds-extension.ts
steps.description: Move listScheduledSeeds + clearScheduledFor out of src/triggers/seeds-extension.ts into a new src/seeds-cli/ module that owns the shell-out facade. Re-export from triggers/ during the move so call sites in src/server/scheduler.ts and src/triggers/tick.ts don't break. Keep SpawnFn injection (mx-371491), SeedsCliError, and the truncate/formatError helpers. No behavior change; tests should pass byte-for-byte once imports are updated.

steps.title: Define warren-owned extensions schema + updateExtensions helper
steps.description: In src/seeds-cli/extensions.ts, add a zod `WarrenSeedExtensionsSchema` covering {role: string, trigger: 'manual'|'cron'|'webhook'|'comment'|'manual-trigger', scheduledFor: string|null, queued: boolean, lastRunId: string, lastRunAt: string, lastScheduledRun: string}. Permissive (.passthrough) on foreign-tool keys per ROADMAP.md:107-108. Export `updateExtensions(deps, projectPath, seedId, patch)` that shells out to `sd update <id> --extensions <json>` — partial patches merge shallowly via seeds semantics. Mirror the SpawnFn shape and timeout handling already in src/triggers/seeds-extension.ts. Returning Result-ish: throws SeedsCliError on non-zero so the spawn caller can downgrade to a system event.

steps.title: Thread seedId through HTTP POST /runs → spawnRun → runs row
steps.description: Add an optional `seedId?: string` to (a) the HTTP POST /runs request schema in src/server/handlers.ts, (b) SpawnRunInput in src/runs/spawn.ts:78, (c) `repos.runs.create` so it persists. Migration: add nullable `seed_id TEXT` column to the runs table (SQLite + Postgres parallel files per R-13). Expose on RunRow + the server Run JSON shape. Triggers/manual-trigger paths (src/server/handlers.ts:354, src/server/scheduler.ts) start passing the seed id they already know. Cancel/steer/reap untouched.

steps.title: Call updateExtensions after successful dispatch in spawnRun
steps.description: In src/runs/spawn.ts after `attachBurrow({burrowRunId})` succeeds (around line 261), if input.seedId is set, fire-and-log `updateExtensions(seedsDeps, projectPath, seedId, {role: agent.name, trigger: input.trigger ?? 'manual', lastRunId: run.id, lastRunAt: now.toISOString()})`. Wrap in try/catch — on failure emit a system event on the run (`event_kind:'seeds_extension_write_failed'`) and continue. The run completing is the source of truth; the seed extension is a denormalized pointer. role intentionally uses agent.name today; R-05 introduces a separate role concept and will update this call site if it diverges. Add a SeedsDepsProvider seam to SpawnRunInput so tests can inject a recording spawn (matches the existing burrowClientPool seam).

steps.title: Consolidate cron tick path onto updateExtensions
steps.description: In src/triggers/tick.ts:184, replace the standalone `clearScheduledFor` write with a single `updateExtensions` call that merges {scheduledFor: null, lastScheduledRun: runId, lastRunId: runId, lastRunAt: now, role: agent.name, trigger: 'cron'} — one shell-out instead of two, idempotent because seeds shallow-merge accepts the same payload twice. Keep the pl-2f15 risk-4 ordering (triggers.last_fired_at stamps BEFORE this call). Update src/triggers/seeds-extension.test.ts assertions and add a case verifying the merged keys.

steps.title: Surface seedId on Run API + minimal RunDetail back-link
steps.description: Add `seedId: string | null` to src/ui/src/api/types.ts Run shape, the server response composer, and src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx header — show a `↩ seeds-xxxx` link next to the existing fields when present. No issues page work here (that's R-04). Goal is just to make the seed binding observable in the UI so an operator inspecting a cron run sees the source seed without grepping events.

steps.title: Acceptance scenario: seeds-extensions-roundtrip
steps.description: Add scripts/acceptance/scenarios/seeds-extensions-roundtrip.ts. Create a project with a seed, POST /runs with `seedId` for the manual path, wait for the run to enter `running`, then `sd show <seed> --format json` and assert extensions.{lastRunId, lastRunAt, role, trigger:'manual'}.
