(c) update warren/CLAUDE.md's brief feature listing to add 'plan-run — serial sd plan execution; activated by the project having a .seeds/ directory'; (d) CHANGELOG.md entry under the next unreleased version following the existing changelog style (look at the v0.3.16 entry for shape); update src/index.ts VERSION constant + package.json version following the dual-bump convention (warren/CLAUDE.md 'Version Management' section); (e) record mulch insights via `ml learn` then `ml record` for any new conventions surfaced (the coordinator state machine, the trivial-merge advance rule, the resume-on-re-dispatch contract — each one a separate record so search-by-domain stays useful, mx-… planning domain); (f) run the full trinity `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` from the warren root AND the acceptance harness (`bun run acceptance --scenario 26` per scripts/acceptance/README if it exists, otherwise the runner the repo uses) before marking the plan done; (g) close warren-fcc9 via `sd close warren-fcc9` then `sd sync && ml sync && git push`. Depends on every prior step; this is the final integration + ship step.

risks: PR-merge polling rate-limits warren's GITHUB_TOKEN under many concurrent active PlanRuns. Mitigation: tick cadence 10s × at most one in-flight child per PlanRun × authenticated GitHub limit 5000/hr means an org running 50 concurrent PlanRuns burns ~1800 calls/hr, well under cap. Coordinator polls only when a child run is in 'pr_open' — not every tick. If the limit is ever hit, the helper's kind:'http_error' result + the per-PlanRun catch keeps the tick alive; operators raise the cadence env or wait it out.

Schema drift between sqlite and postgres dialects after hand-editing migrations. Mitigation: run src/db/schema/drift.test.ts after every schema change (mx-de2250) — it imports both dialect schemas via drizzle-kit's getTableConfig and asserts column-list + index-list parity. The cross-dialect row-passthrough pattern (mx-845f11) relies on this. Also rerun migrations.test.ts which boots both dialects against the generated SQL.

Coordinator races with bridge: the bridge mirrors queued→running but reap owns terminal transitions (mx-30a49a / mx-98a470). The coordinator must not read child.run.state directly without going through repos.runs.get which reflects only writer transitions. Mitigation: read run rows via repos.runs.get exclusively; never read live burrow state from the coordinator. The state machine in step 5 is keyed on the warren-side runs row only. The reap transition is the moment a child becomes eligible to advance (terminal state observable); the bridge's queued→running mirror is irrelevant to the coordinator.

Resume-on-re-dispatch double-spawns a child whose seed was closed by the previous attempt but whose plan_run_children row is stuck in 'dispatched' because warren crashed mid-flight. Mitigation: on PlanRun creation, the coordinator's first tick MUST call showSeed for every child whose state is 'pending' AND for every child currently in 'dispatched'/'running' state on the PREVIOUS PlanRun for the same planId+projectId, transitioning closed seeds to 'skipped' BEFORE attempting any new dispatch. Persist this 'crash recovery sweep' explicitly in step 2's PlanRunsRepo (a `reconcile(planRunId, seedStatuses)` method) and exercise it via scenario 26 step (8).

Sequential semantic breaks when an operator manually opens a stacked PR between PlanRun children, advancing main past the previous child's merge. Mitigation: acceptable — each spawn calls refreshProjectClone which fast-forwards to remote main before the child workspace is provisioned (mx-1bb6). The child branches off whatever main is at dispatch time, NOT off the previous child's merge commit specifically. Document this in SPEC §11.P explicitly so operators know stacked-merge interleavings during a PlanRun are tolerated, not corrupted.

showPlan output schema drifts as seeds adds new plan fields, breaking the parser. Mitigation: the Zod schema uses .passthrough() at every level (matches the existing SeedExtensionsSchema posture in src/seeds-cli/schema.ts) so unknown fields are tolerated. Only the fields warren reads are required. Lock the contract with a unit test that parses a real `sd plan show pl-2047 --json` capture committed under src/seeds-cli/__fixtures__/.

PostgreSQL CHECK constraint on plan_run_state would require a migration when we add a new state. Mitigation: don't use SQL CHECK constraints — TS-only narrowing via the enum tuple in src/db/schema/columns.ts (mx-2ab984). Drizzle's `text({enum})` is TS-only by design; new states ship as a one-line tuple change + a migration only for index additions, not state expansions.
acceptance: POST /plan-runs against a project with .seeds/ + a valid sd plan id returns 201 with state='queued' and one plan_run_children row per open child in plan-order; against a project without .seeds/ rejects 400 with code:'project_lacks_seeds'; against a non-existent plan or one with no open children rejects 400 with the typed error code.

An end-to-end run of acceptance scenario 26 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/26-plan-run-roundtrip.ts) succeeds: three children dispatch in order, each waiting for the prior's PR merge (or trivial-merge for zero-commit children), the PlanRun terminates with state='succeeded', and re-dispatching the same plan id after manually closing one child skips it via the 'skipped' state without re-running the agent.

A child run dispatched by the coordinator has trigger='plan-run', seedId=<the child's seed>, and metadata.planRunId pointing at the parent; existing seedId-driven extension wiring (pl-bb70) stamps {role, trigger:'plan-run', lastRunId, lastRunAt} on the seed via sd update --extensions — verified by scenario 26 reading the project's .seeds/issues.jsonl post-completion.

Single-run dispatch (POST /runs) is byte-identical to the warren-fcc9 baseline — diffing the pre/post create response payloads for an identical input produces no field changes; the existing /runs handler tests in src/server/handlers.test.ts pass without modification.

Boot the server with WARREN_PLAN_RUN_DISABLED=1 and POST /plan-runs still inserts the row (the API surface is independent of the coordinator); without the disable, the coordinator tick advances within `WARREN_PLAN_RUN_TICK_MS` of dispatch — verified via the coordinator unit tests in src/plan-runs/tick.test.ts.

POST /plan-runs/:id/cancel during an in-flight child transitions the PlanRun to 'cancelled' AND issues cancelRun against the in-flight child; an already-terminal PlanRun returns the unchanged state with `alreadyTerminal:true`.

src/db/schema/drift.test.ts and src/db/migrations/migrations.test.ts pass after the new schema lands; the postgres migration mirror exists and the rls migration pattern (0002_enable_rls.sql, mx-889760) is updated to grant RLS on the two new tables.

`bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` succeeds from the warren root with zero warnings (biome --error-on-warnings is the gate, warren/CLAUDE.md 'Quality Gates'); the new acceptance scenario 26 runs green in the in-proc mode.

Documentation lands: SPEC.md §11.P exists and references the coordinator state machine + env vars; README.md and CLAUDE.md mention plan-run as a `.seeds/`-gated dispatch mode; CHANGELOG.md has a new entry; package.json + src/index.ts VERSION are bumped consistently (warren/CLAUDE.md 'Version Management' both-files-or-CI-fails).

sections.context: warren-06dc is Phase 2 of the Plot adoption epic (warren-000b). Phase 1 (pl-2047, closed) wired Plot into single-run dispatch — `runs.plot_id` column, `PLOT_ID`/`PLOT_ACTOR` env injection at spawn (src/runs/spawn.ts:671), `run_dispatched` event emission via `defaultPlotAppender` (spawn.ts:590), and JSONL-tail-at-reap mirroring (src/runs/reap.ts:1259). PlanRun V1 (pl-a258, just landed) built a serial plan-run coordinator on top of the single-run primitive (src/plan-runs/coordinator.ts) but explicitly deferred Plot wiring (warren-a87f: 'NO Plot wiring; warren-06dc owns Phase 2'). This plan adds the same wiring onto PlanRun so a plan-run launched from a Plot threads context through every child and reports lifecycle back to the Plot. Composition is purely additive (per warren-fcc9 acceptance: 'existing single-run dispatch path is unchanged'). Because the coordinator already routes every child through `spawnRun` (src/plan-runs/dispatch.ts), the per-child `run_dispatched` and PLOT_ID/PLOT_ACTOR injection light up automatically once the coordinator forwards `plot_id` into the spawn input — most of the work is plumbing one nullable field through the DB, API, coordinator, and UI. The two non-trivial pieces are (1) a new `plan_run_dispatched` Plot event for the parent PlanRun lifecycle (blocked on plot-3e3d to land in the @os-eco/plot-cli library since `PLOT_EVENT_TYPES` is a closed enum) and (2) auto-transitioning the Plot from `active` → `done` when all PlanRun children close, which uses `user:<dispatcherHandle>` to satisfy plot's `status_changed: ['user']` ACL.
sections.approach: Mirror Phase 1's pl-2047 shape end-to-end on the PlanRun surface, never forking the single-run code path. (a) Add a nullable `plot_id` column to `plan_runs` in BOTH dialect schemas (sqlite + postgres) following the exact shape of `runs.plot_id` (warren-a8c3 / src/db/schema/sqlite.ts:155), with index `plan_runs_plot_id_idx` registered in src/db/schema/columns.ts (mx-de2250 drift test). (b) Extend `POST /plan-runs` (src/server/handlers.ts:1229) to accept optional `plot_id`; reject with a typed `project_lacks_plot` error when `plot_id` is set but `project.hasPlot` is false, mirroring the `ProjectLacksSeedsError` shape already in src/plan-runs/errors.ts (defensive narrowing at the handler edge, no half-row state). (c) Thread `plot_id` through `CoordinatorSpawnFn` (src/plan-runs/coordinator.ts:62) into the dispatch wrapper (src/plan-runs/dispatch.ts) so each child run gets spawned with `plotId: planRun.plotId`; this means existing PLOT_ID/PLOT_ACTOR injection in `composePlotEnv` (spawn.ts:671) and per-child `run_dispatched` emission via `defaultPlotAppender` (spawn.ts:590) light up for free, with zero changes to the single-run path. (d) Emit one `plan_run_dispatched` Plot event at PlanRun row creation in the handler, mirroring `defaultPlotAppender`'s rebuild-index + retry-once + best-effort-log shape (spawn.ts:590–622); a new `defaultPlanRunPlotAppender` helper lives in src/plan-runs/ alongside the coordinator, fired right after `repos.planRuns.create`. The actor is `user:<dispatcherHandle>` (PlanRun row already stores this; defaults to 'operator' per pl-a258 / mx-77f5ac), satisfying plot's ACL (`run_dispatched`/`plan_run_dispatched` both allow user actors). (e) Auto-transition the Plot from `active` → `done` when the coordinator transitions a `plan_runs` row to `succeeded`. Add a `transitionPlotToDone` hook called from coordinator.ts inside the `plan_succeeded` arm: fetch the bound Plot via UserPlotClient with actor `user:<dispatcherHandle>`, guard on `plot.status === 'active'` (don't trample `done`/`archived`/`drafting`/`ready`), call `setStatus('done')` — best-effort, log a `plot_auto_done_failed` warren event on failure (mirror `recordPlotAppendFailure` in spawn.ts:534). (f) UI: add a free-form optional `plot_id` text input to NewPlanRun.tsx after the `planId` input; render the `plot_id` on PlanRunDetail.tsx with a placeholder link to `/plots/:id` (Phase 3 owns the Plot detail page so a dead-link href is acceptable per warren-d362 sequencing — file a follow-up for live linking). (g) Acceptance scenario 27 composes scenario 25 (Plot dispatch) + scenario 26 (PlanRun roundtrip): three children, one trivial-merge case, assert PLOT_ID lands in every child sandbox, `plan_run_dispatched` lands once at PlanRun start, per-child `run_dispatched` events land via the unchanged Phase 1 path, Plot auto-transitions to `done` after the final child merges, and re-dispatching the same plan id against a project without `.plot/` is byte-identical to the pl-a258 baseline (no Plot leakage). Gating: surface `plot_id` only when BOTH `.plot/` AND `.seeds/` are present (PlanRun's existing gate stacked with Plot's gate); don't loosen either. The plan is blocked on plot-3e3d (filed in plot/) landing in a published `@os-eco/plot-cli` version warren can pin — that lands the `plan_run_dispatched` type in `PLOT_EVENT_TYPES` + ACL, which warren must consume before step 4 can wire the appender.

alternatives.name: Reuse `run_dispatched` with a discriminator payload instead of a new `plan_run_dispatched` event type
alternatives.rejected_because: The seed explicitly asks for a distinct `plan_run_dispatched` event (warren-06dc §Scope: 'A single plan_run_dispatched event on the Plot when the PlanRun itself starts'). Reusing run_dispatched would conflate two semantically different surfaces — a child run vs. the parent PlanRun — and make Plot consumers (CLI formatter, future Plot detail UI in warren-d362) handle the discriminator in N places. The cross-repo cost is one enum line + one ACL entry + a plot point release (plot-3e3d), which the user committed to knocking out quickly. Worth the round-trip for type-system legibility downstream.

alternatives.name: Add a synthetic `system:warren` or `user:warren-auto` actor for the auto-done transition instead of using `user:<dispatcherHandle>`
alternatives.rejected_because: Plot's ACL (plot/src/acl.ts:17) hardcodes `status_changed: ['user']` and has no `system` ActorKind. Adding one would force a parallel plot/ seed bigger than plot-3e3d (cross-cutting ACL change) and break the 'agents are bad at intent, humans own status' framing in plot SPEC §6. `user:<dispatcherHandle>` (PlanRun already stores this, defaults to 'operator') frames the auto-transition as 'warren acting on behalf of the operator who dispatched the PlanRun' — mirrors how Phase 1 attributes `run_dispatched` to the dispatcher (spawn.ts:594). Operators see who kicked off the plan whose completion auto-transitioned their Plot.

alternatives.name: Fire `plan_run_dispatched` from the coordinator's first tick instead of from the POST /plan-runs handler
alternatives.rejected_because: Coordinator ticks have a single-flight guard (mx-fb4a85) and can be racy on first tick after creation. The handler already does all the validation work (project.hasPlot check, plan existence, child enumeration) and runs at most once per PlanRun creation. Firing the event right after `repos.planRuns.create` keeps event-time close to wall-clock-time of the operator action, mirrors Phase 1's posture (spawn.ts:407–425 fires immediately post-spawn, not from a later tick), and isolates Plot-write failures from coordinator state. The handler is the right seam for a one-shot lifecycle event.

alternatives.name: Block PlanRun dispatch on the bound Plot being in `active` status
alternatives.rejected_because: The seed places no constraint on Plot status at dispatch time — only on the auto-done transition target. Operators may dispatch a PlanRun against a Plot in `drafting`/`ready` (e.g. to attach the run before formally activating) or in `done`/`archived` (e.g. historical re-run for verification). Enforcing status would surprise operators and trip up flows the Plot detail page (warren-d362 Phase 3b) hasn't designed yet. The auto-done transition's `status === 'active'` guard is sufficient — non-active Plots simply don't auto-transition.

alternatives.name: Skip the per-child `run_dispatched` events because PlanRun already emits `plan_run_dispatched`
alternatives.rejected_because: Phase 3 UI (warren-d362) builds a unified activity feed where agent runs appear alongside human events; suppressing per-child `run_dispatched` would hide entire runs from the Plot's timeline. The per-child events are Phase 1's contract (warren-e848); PlanRun composition is additive — the `plan_run_dispatched` event is one parent event, NOT a replacement. They serve different timeline roles.

alternatives.name: Persist `plot_id` as a JSON column on the existing `dispatcher_handle` row, or in a separate sidecar table
alternatives.rejected_because: Single nullable indexed column matches Phase 1's `runs.plot_id` exactly (warren-a8c3); querying 'PlanRuns bound to plot X' is one indexed scan. Both alternatives — JSON-blob or sidecar table — add joins or JSON path ops for zero benefit. The Phase 3 UI will need exactly this query.

alternatives.name: Have the coordinator setStatus('done') only after every child PR is also visible in the Plot's event log (verify before transitioning)
alternatives.rejected_because: Per-child `run_dispatched` events are already best-effort (Phase 1 / warren-e848: 'failure to append does NOT fail the spawn but is logged as a warren event'). If a transient Plot append failed mid-PlanRun, the operator can rebuild the Plot's index and re-emit if needed — but blocking the auto-done on Plot-side verification creates a dependency loop (you need a Plot-write to verify Plot-writes) and would deadlock the PlanRun's terminal state. Best-effort posture is consistent with Phase 1.

steps.title: Bump @os-eco/plot-cli to the version that lands plan_run_dispatched
steps.description: Blocked on plot-3e3d landing a published `@os-eco/plot-cli` minor that adds `plan_run_dispatched` to `PLOT_EVENT_TYPES` (plot/src/types.ts) and `EVENT_ACL.plan_run_dispatched = ['user', 'agent']` (plot/src/acl.ts). Update warren's pin in BOTH places per the burrow-cli double-pin rule (CLAUDE.md 'Relationship to burrow' / warren-8f64 plot-cli mirror): (a) package.json `dependencies` field, (b) bun.lock (regenerate via `bun install`), (c) Dockerfile global install line that mirrors the burrow-cli global install (verify the existing plot-cli global install line from warren-8f64). Run `bun run build` and `bun run build:ui` to confirm no tsc/bun-types ImportMeta.hot duplicate-declaration trap (mx-ef8eab / pl-2047 risk #8) — if the new plot-cli minor pulls a refreshed bun-types subdep, the tsconfig exclude pattern may need extending. Verify the type-level export by writing a one-line probe in src/plot-client/index.ts that does `import { PLOT_EVENT_TYPES } from '@os-eco/plot-cli'; PLOT_EVENT_TYPES.includes('plan_run_dispatched');` and confirm tsc accepts it. NO behavioral change in this step — just the dep bump and a compile-time proof the new type is reachable. Run `bun test src/plot-client && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` before closing. This step gates every downstream step that needs to construct a `plan_run_dispatched` event (step 4 onwards).

steps.title: Add plan_runs.plot_id column + migration + repo plumbing
steps.description: Add a nullable `plot_id TEXT` column to the `plan_runs` table in BOTH dialect schemas: src/db/schema/sqlite.ts (around the existing `plan_runs` block at lines 329–354 added by warren-4d7c) and src/db/schema/postgres.ts (matching block). Mirror the exact shape of `runs.plot_id` (warren-a8c3): nullable, no default, no foreign key. Add an index `plan_runs_plot_id_idx ON plan_runs(plot_id)` in both dialects (mirror the index pattern used for `runs_plot_id_idx` at src/db/schema/sqlite.ts:198). Register the index name in src/db/schema/columns.ts INDEX_NAMES (mx-de2250 — the drift test src/db/schema/drift.test.ts imports from this file). Regenerate sqlite + postgres migrations via `bun run --silent drizzle-kit generate --config drizzle.config.sqlite.ts` and the postgres counterpart (mx-ddc0ec), then hand-rename to descriptive `0016_add_plan_runs_plot_id.sql` / `0009_add_plan_runs_plot_id.sql` per the rename convention (mx-b4f9b5). Update src/db/repos/plan-runs.ts: extend `PlanRunRow` row type to include `plotId: string | null` (mirror the partial-input pattern from RunsRepo.attachBurrow / mx-0a7a65), extend `create({planRunRow, children})` to persist it, extend `getById` / `listByProjectAndState` / `listActive` SELECT lists to surface it (drizzle's typed `select()` from the column-augmented schema does this automatically once the schema gains the column — verify nothing manually deselects it). Extend src/db/repos/plan-runs.test.ts (added by warren-4d7c) with two cases: (a) `create` persists `plotId` when provided, (b) `create` persists `plotId: null` when omitted. Run `bun test src/db/repos/plan-runs src/db/schema src/db/migrations && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` and confirm src/db/schema/drift.test.ts + src/db/migrations/migrations.test.ts both pass without further hand-editing. The RLS migration pattern (0002_enable_rls.sql, mx-889760) does NOT need updating — RLS is already on the table; adding a column inherits the existing grant. This step's substrate-only change blocks step 3 (which validates `plot_id` at the handler) and step 4 (which writes the event using the persisted column).

steps.title: Extend POST /plan-runs to accept and validate plot_id
steps.description: Update the POST /plan-runs handler in src/server/handlers.ts (lines 1229–1325, added by warren-f923) to accept an optional `plotId` field on the request body. Validation order: (1) existing project lookup + 404; (2) existing `!project.hasSeeds` → ProjectLacksSeedsError check (unchanged); (3) NEW: if `body.plotId !== undefined && body.plotId !== '' && !project.hasPlot` → throw a typed error matching the runs handler's posture (src/runs/spawn.ts:257–265). Define a new `ProjectLacksPlotError extends ValidationError` in src/plan-runs/errors.ts mirroring `ProjectLacksSeedsError` (same constructor signature, code `'project_lacks_plot'`, recoveryHint `'either omit plot_id on POST /plan-runs, or run plot init in the project clone and refresh the project so warren picks up the .plot/ directory'`). (4) existing plan existence + open-children check (unchanged); (5) existing agent resolution (unchanged); (6) build the plan_runs row with `plotId: body.plotId ?? null`; persist via `repos.planRuns.create`; return 201 with the full row including plotId. Update the request body type in src/server/types.ts (if separate) to add optional `plotId?: string`. Update GET /plan-runs/:id response shape to surface `plotId` (already covered by the schema-driven select in step 2, verify the typed payload includes it). Update GET /plan-runs list response similarly. Update src/server/handlers.plan-runs.test.ts (the suite added by warren-f923 — or equivalent location) with three cases: (a) POST with plotId on hasPlot=true project succeeds and persists, (b) POST with plotId on hasPlot=false project rejects 400 with code:'project_lacks_plot', (c) POST omitting plotId on hasPlot=true project succeeds with plotId=null. Add the new ProjectLacksPlotError → 400 mapping in the existing error->status switch (mirror the ProjectLacksSeedsError handling — same envelope shape per mx-49a44c). NO change to the single-run POST /runs path or the existing PlanRun cancel/get/events handlers. Depends on step 2 (plotId column). Blocks step 4 (the event appender needs the persisted row) and step 7 (UI form posts the new field).

steps.title: Emit plan_run_dispatched on PlanRun creation (best-effort, post-persist)
steps.description: Add a new helper `defaultPlanRunPlotAppender` in src/plan-runs/ (suggest src/plan-runs/plot.ts; co-locate with the coordinator) that mirrors `defaultPlotAppender` in src/runs/spawn.ts:590–622 exactly: opens a `UserPlotClient` with `actor: { kind: 'user', handle: planRun.dispatcherHandle, raw: \`user:${planRun.dispatcherHandle}\` }`, calls `plot.append({ type: 'plan_run_dispatched', data: { plan_run_id, plan_id, children_count } })`, on first failure invokes `client.rebuildIndex()` best-effort and retries the append once. The signature: `appendPlanRunDispatched({ plotDir: string, plotId: string, handle: string, planRunId: string, planId: string, childrenCount: number })`. Wire into POST /plan-runs handler in src/server/handlers.ts: immediately AFTER `repos.planRuns.create` succeeds AND when `planRunRow.plotId !== null`, call the appender. Wrap the call in try/catch and route failures to a new `recordPlanRunPlotAppendFailure` helper (mirror `recordPlotAppendFailure` in src/runs/spawn.ts:534–566) that appends a `plan_run.plot_append_failed` event to the warren event stream on the PlanRun row itself (not on a child run — there is no child run yet at creation time). The PlanRun row's `id` is the event subject; use the existing events repo seam. Failure to append does NOT fail the POST /plan-runs response — the row is already persisted and the operator gets a 201. Wire the appender into ServerDeps (src/server/types.ts) as `planRunPlotAppender: PlanRunPlotAppender` so handler tests can stub it (mirror how `spawnPlotAppender` is wired). Add unit tests in src/plan-runs/plot.test.ts: (a) happy path appends with correct payload + actor, (b) first-attempt error triggers rebuildIndex + retry, (c) sustained failure throws (so the handler can route to recordPlanRunPlotAppendFailure). Add a handler test case in handlers.plan-runs.test.ts: POST /plan-runs with plotId set → stub appender called once with the correct shape; stub failing → 201 still returned AND a `plan_run.plot_append_failed` event lands. Mocking the plot library follows the same pattern src/runs/spawn.test.ts uses for spawnPlotAppender. Depends on step 1 (the `plan_run_dispatched` type must exist in @os-eco/plot-cli), step 2 (plotId column to read off the row), step 3 (handler accepts plotId). Blocks step 7 (UI may want to surface the event in the detail page) and step 8 (acceptance scenario asserts the event lands).

steps.title: Thread plot_id through coordinator → child spawn, then auto-transition Plot to done
steps.description: Two changes in the coordinator, both purely additive to the state machine: (A) Pass plot_id through the spawn seam. Update `CoordinatorSpawnInput` in src/plan-runs/coordinator.ts:52 to include `plotId: string | null` derived from `planRun.plotId`; update the dispatch wrapper src/plan-runs/dispatch.ts so the closure that constructs the spawnRun input includes `plotId: input.plotId`. spawnRun (src/runs/spawn.ts) already handles plot_id end-to-end (validation at line 257, env injection at 671, run_dispatched emission at 590) — no change to spawnRun itself. This means each child run dispatched by the coordinator gets PLOT_ID + PLOT_ACTOR=agent:<agent>:<child-run-id> in its sandbox env automatically, AND a per-child `run_dispatched` event lands on the Plot for free. The trivial-merge case (mx-fd8619) is unaffected — empty-push children still emit run_dispatched at spawn time; only the merge happens trivially. (B) Auto-transition Plot active → done on plan success. In src/plan-runs/coordinator.ts, locate the `plan_succeeded` arm (the branch that calls `repos.planRuns.transitionTo(planRun.id, 'succeeded', { endedAt })`); AFTER that transition lands AND when `planRun.plotId !== null`, call a new `transitionPlotToDone` helper. Add the helper in src/plan-runs/plot.ts (alongside step 4's appender): opens a UserPlotClient with actor `user:<planRun.dispatcherHandle>`, calls `client.get(plotId)` to read current status, gates on `plot.status === 'active'` (other statuses noop and emit a `plan_run.plot_status_skipped` warren event for observability), calls `handle.setStatus('done')` (plot's handle.ts:83 surface). Best-effort: on any failure (Plot missing, status not active, ACL throw, fs error) log a `plan_run.plot_auto_done_failed` warren event on the planRun's id and continue. Do NOT block the plan_succeeded return on the Plot write — the PlanRun is already in 'succeeded' state. The status check uses the planRun.plotId resolved at PlanRun creation, NOT a live re-read of any operator-edited target. Unit tests in src/plan-runs/plot.test.ts (extends step 4's file): (a) active Plot → setStatus called with 'done', (b) drafting/ready/done/archived Plot → no setStatus, plan_run.plot_status_skipped event, (c) setStatus throws → plan_run.plot_auto_done_failed event, coordinator continues. Unit tests in src/plan-runs/coordinator.test.ts (added by warren-2623): extend the existing `plan_succeeded` branch test with two new cases — (a) plotId=null → no transitionPlotToDone call (purely the pl-a258 baseline), (b) plotId set + Plot active → transitionPlotToDone called once. Wire the helper into the coordinator deps shape (extend `AdvancePlanRunInput` in coordinator.ts:93 with `plotTransitioner: PlotTransitionerFn` — keep the existing optional shape so tests can stub it). Wire the production transitioner into `bootPlanRunCoordinator` in src/plan-runs/tick.ts:165 (mirror how showSeed and checkPrMerged are wired) and into the boot call in src/server/main.ts:262. Depends on steps 1, 2, 3. Blocks step 8 (acceptance scenario asserts the auto-done transition + per-child PLOT_ID env).

steps.title: Restate gating: stack .plot/ on .seeds/ at the handler edge
steps.description: Belt-and-suspenders defensive check that the PlanRun + Plot feature surface is gated correctly. The handler-level rejection in step 3 already rejects `plot_id without hasPlot`, and the existing PlanRun gate already requires `hasSeeds`. This step audits and documents the stack and adds one combined test. (a) Add a test case in handlers.plan-runs.test.ts: POST /plan-runs with `plotId` set against a project where `hasSeeds=true AND hasPlot=false` → 400 with code:'project_lacks_plot' (validates step 3's rejection takes precedence appropriately when both flags might apply). (b) Add a test case: POST /plan-runs without `plotId` against `hasSeeds=true AND hasPlot=false` succeeds normally (the absence of plot_id means no plot involvement at all — PlanRun is .seeds-only). (c) Document the gate-stacking in src/plan-runs/README.md (create if absent — short module-level README mirroring src/runs/README.md if it exists, or just inline in src/plan-runs/index.ts top-of-file doc comment). The doc states: 'PlanRun requires .seeds/; Plot-bound PlanRun additionally requires .plot/; both gates are stacked at POST /plan-runs and never loosened.' (d) Verify the existing handler ordering produces ProjectLacksSeedsError BEFORE ProjectLacksPlotError when both gates fail — mirror the runs-handler ordering convention so operator error messages surface the more foundational missing dep first. Adjust step 3's order if needed (the simpler is foundational, plot is composed). (e) Run `bun test src/server/handlers.plan-runs && bun run lint && bun run typecheck`. This step is small and could be folded into step 3 in review but tracking it separately keeps the gate-stacking decision auditable. Depends on step 3. Does not block anything downstream.

steps.title: Surface plot_id in the NewPlanRun form and PlanRunDetail page
steps.description: Three UI changes in src/ui/, all isolated to PlanRun pages — no change to single-run pages, no nav changes. (a) Extend the API client + types: in src/ui/src/api/client.ts, add `plotId?: string | null` to the `CreatePlanRunInput` type (mirror the server's POST body shape from step 3); in src/ui/src/api/types.ts, add `plotId: string | null` to `PlanRunRow` (mirror RunRow.plotId already added by warren-a8c3) and to `PlanRunDetailResponse`. (b) NewPlanRun.tsx: add an optional `plot_id` text input below the existing `planId` input (mirror the existing planId input shape; same Tailwind classes; placeholder 'plot-XXXXXXXX (optional)'); wire to a new `plotId` form-state slot; submit empty-string as `undefined` (don't post empty strings). Conditionally render the input ONLY when the selected project's `hasPlot` is true (read off `ProjectRow.hasPlot` already added by warren-4e20); when `hasPlot` is false, hide the input entirely (do not show a 'Plot runs require .plot/' banner — the field is optional, not required, so silence is correct). Surface a single inline error if the server returns code:'project_lacks_plot' (rare, since the field is conditionally hidden, but defensive). (c) PlanRunDetail.tsx: in the header section (state badge, plan_id, agent, etc., added by warren-a87f), add a 'Plot' row showing the plotId as a placeholder link `<Link to={`/plots/${plotId}`}>` rendered with a tooltip 'Plot detail page coming in Phase 3' since /plots/:id is owned by warren-d362 and may not exist yet. The link href is correct future-shaped; the destination route is a known dead-end for V1 (file a follow-up seed to wire live linking when /plots/:id lands). When plotId is null, omit the 'Plot' row entirely. No change to the child table or Cancel button. (d) PlanRuns.tsx list page: NO change for V1 — keep the existing columns added by warren-a87f. Plot-binding visibility lives on the detail page, not the list (the list already has child-count columns and adding plotId would crowd the row). File a follow-up seed if the Phase 3 UI work needs a plot-id column on the list. (e) Run `bun run build:ui && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` from the warren root. NO new vitest tests (sibling PlanRun pages have none — per the warren-a87f decision to keep PlanRun UI test-free for V1; consistent here). Depends on steps 3 and 4 (the API surface and event shape). Blocks step 8 (the acceptance scenario asserts the UI POSTs the plotId correctly, though most of step 8 is server-side).

steps.title: Acceptance scenario 27: PlanRun + Plot roundtrip end-to-end
steps.description: Create scripts/acceptance/scenarios/27-plan-run-plot-roundtrip.ts modeled on scenarios 25 (Plot dispatch, scripts/acceptance/scenarios/25-plot-roundtrip.ts) and 26 (PlanRun, scripts/acceptance/scenarios/26-plan-run-roundtrip.ts — the closest twin). Steps: (1) ensureSampleProject + reset source state (idempotent per mx-895738). (2) Bootstrap the project with BOTH `.seeds/` and `.plot/` directories: copy the scenario 26 seeds bootstrap (`.seeds/config.yaml` + three seeds ah-acc-27-a/b/c, where c is docs-only/zero-commit to drive the trivial-merge branch) and the scenario 25 plot bootstrap (initialize a Plot via `plot init` server-side OR write the JSON+JSONL directly using the same posture scenario 25 takes); commit + refresh. The new Plot should be in `status: 'active'` so the auto-done transition target is met. (3) Commit a `.seeds/plans.jsonl` row pl-acc-27 with children [ah-acc-27-a, ah-acc-27-b, ah-acc-27-c] and status='approved' (same write-direct posture as scenario 26 step 3). (4) POST /plan-runs with {project, planId:'pl-acc-27', agent:<acceptance stub agent>, promptTemplate:'closeseed {seed_id}', plotId:<the bootstrapped Plot id>}; assert 201 + state='queued' + 3 child rows in 'pending' + plotId persisted. (5) Assert immediately: read .plot/pl-<id>.events.jsonl, find a `plan_run_dispatched` event with payload {plan_run_id, plan_id:'pl-acc-27', children_count:3} and actor=`user:operator` (the default dispatcherHandle). (6) Poll GET /plan-runs/:id every 1s up to 60s deadline; for each child, the acceptance stub agent (scripts/acceptance/lib/stub-agent/agent.sh) gets a parameterized prompt — pass `closeseed <id>` for children a/b (with commits) and `closeseed <id> --no-commit` for c (trivial-merge); inject `WARREN_GH_FETCH_OVERRIDE` (added by warren-ae00 / scenario 26) so checkPullRequestMerged returns kind:'merged' immediately. (7) Assert per-child env: the stub agent dumps `printenv PLOT_ID PLOT_ACTOR` into an artifact in `.warren/acceptance-env-dumps/<run-id>.env` (mirror the scenario 25 env-dump pattern); for each of the 3 child runs, verify PLOT_ID=<the Plot id> and PLOT_ACTOR=`agent:<agent-name>:<child-run-id>`. (8) Assert per-child events: read .plot/pl-<id>.events.jsonl after each child reaps, assert one `run_dispatched` event per child with payload.run_id matching the child run row. (9) Assert terminal state: plan_runs row state='succeeded'; all three plan_run_children state='merged' (last via trivial-merge); the Plot's `status` is now 'done' (re-read via `plot get` or by parsing the Plot's JSON file); the Plot's event log has a `status_changed` event with actor=`user:operator` and data={from:'active', to:'done'}. (10) Negative test: POST /plan-runs against a project with `.seeds/` but NO `.plot/`, passing plotId='plot-fake' → assert 400 with code:'project_lacks_plot' and no row inserted (scan plan_runs table by planId). (11) Byte-identity test: POST /plan-runs against a `.seeds/`-only project WITHOUT plotId → assert the entire response payload diff against the scenario 26 baseline is empty for the matching fields (no Plot leakage; serialize both and compare). (12) Idempotent cleanup: scenario 27 cleans up its source-repo seed rows + Plot directory + planRun rows (mirror scenario 26 teardown). Modes: ['in-proc'] only (matches scenarios 25/26 — drives source-repo edits the container harness doesn't bind-mount, mx-1d31f0). Verify `bun run acceptance --scenario 27` passes; verify scenarios 25 and 26 still pass (no regressions). Depends on every preceding step. Blocks step 9.

steps.title: SPEC + docs + CHANGELOG + version bump + ml records
steps.description: (a) Extend warren/SPEC.md §11.P (PlanRun: serial plan execution, added by warren-7401) with a new subsection §11.P.Plot 'Plot composition (Phase 2)' covering: the `plan_runs.plot_id` column + gating (.plot/ + .seeds/ stacked), per-child PLOT_ID/PLOT_ACTOR inheritance via the existing single-run path, the `plan_run_dispatched` event contract (emitted once at PlanRun creation, payload {plan_run_id, plan_id, children_count}, actor `user:<dispatcherHandle>`, best-effort + warren-event-log on failure), the auto-done transition (gated on plot.status==='active', uses dispatcherHandle, non-active Plots noop with observability event), the cross-repo dep on plot-3e3d's `plan_run_dispatched` event type, and the stacked-gate ordering (project_lacks_seeds before project_lacks_plot). Cross-reference §11.O (Plot integration / pl-2047) and clarify that Phase 2 reuses Phase 1's env-injection + event-mirror substrate without forking it. (b) Update warren/README.md: under the feature list, mention that PlanRun composes with Plot — surface the example dispatch shape `warren plan-run pl-XXXX --plot plot-XXXXXXXX` (or whatever the CLI surface is; check the existing PlanRun CLI section). Keep the standalone path primary; Plot wiring is presented as a feature that lights up when both `.plot/` and `.seeds/` are present in the project. (c) Update warren/CLAUDE.md's brief feature listing: extend the line about plan-run with 'optionally composable with Plot when .plot/ is also present in the project — see SPEC §11.P.Plot'. (d) CHANGELOG.md: add an entry under the next unreleased version following the existing style (look at the most recent entry for shape — likely v0.3.17 or whatever was bumped by warren-7401). Mention: 'PlanRun + Plot composition: optional plot_id on POST /plan-runs threads PLOT_ID/PLOT_ACTOR through every child run, emits plan_run_dispatched on the bound Plot, and auto-transitions Plot status from active to done on plan success.' (e) Dual-bump version per warren/CLAUDE.md 'Version Management': edit BOTH package.json `version` AND src/index.ts `VERSION` constant; CI release workflow asserts the dual matches. (f) Record mulch insights via `ml learn` then `ml record planning` for the three new conventions: (1) the stacked-gate ordering (project_lacks_seeds before project_lacks_plot), (2) the auto-done transition pattern (user:<dispatcherHandle> + status==='active' gate + best-effort observability), (3) the cross-repo dep choreography (plot/ landing the event type before warren can pin and consume it). Each as a separate record so search-by-domain stays useful. (g) Run the full trinity from warren/ root: `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck`. Run the acceptance harness for scenario 27 AND verify scenarios 25 + 26 still pass (no regressions). (h) Close warren-06dc via `sd close warren-06dc` then `sd sync && ml sync && git push`. Depends on every prior step.

risks: Cross-repo timing: plot-3e3d must publish a `@os-eco/plot-cli` minor adding `plan_run_dispatched` to PLOT_EVENT_TYPES + EVENT_ACL before this plan can complete step 1. Mitigation: step 1 is intentionally the foundation block — every downstream step that constructs `plan_run_dispatched` events is gated on the bumped dep. If plot-3e3d slips, warren-06dc execution pauses at step 1 cleanly without polluting downstream branches. Add a compile-time probe in step 1 that fails the typecheck if PLOT_EVENT_TYPES does not include 'plan_run_dispatched'.

Double-pin drift on @os-eco/plot-cli (package.json + bun.lock + Dockerfile global install). Mitigation: same risk pl-2047 already mitigated for the initial plot-cli addition (warren-8f64). Step 1 must touch all three pin locations and the release workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml) should fail on mismatch. This is the burrow-cli / plot-cli double-pin convention captured in warren/CLAUDE.md 'Relationship to burrow'.

Schema drift between sqlite and postgres dialects after hand-editing migrations. Mitigation: run src/db/schema/drift.test.ts after every schema change (mx-de2250) — it imports both dialect schemas via drizzle-kit's getTableConfig and asserts column-list + index-list parity. Also rerun migrations.test.ts which boots both dialects against the generated SQL. Step 2 explicitly runs both.

Auto-done transition races with operator manually transitioning Plot status mid-run. Operator manually moves Plot from `active` to `done` (or `archived`) while the coordinator advances children: when the coordinator finally reaches `plan_succeeded`, the Plot is no longer in `active`. Mitigation: the gate `plot.status === 'active'` makes this a clean noop with a `plan_run.plot_status_skipped` warren event for observability — no throw, no clobber. Document in SPEC §11.P.Plot that the transition is best-effort by design.

Trivial-merge child + Plot append: per-child `run_dispatched` must still land even when commitsAhead=0. Mitigation: Phase 1's `defaultPlotAppender` in src/runs/spawn.ts:590 fires at spawn time BEFORE reap evaluates commitsAhead, so the per-child run_dispatched is independent of merge outcome. Scenario 27 step 8 explicitly asserts run_dispatched lands for the trivial-merge child (ah-acc-27-c) — covers this exact case.

dispatcherHandle defaulting to 'operator' attributes many auto-done transitions to a synthetic handle. Mitigation: acceptable for V1 — the operator who dispatched the PlanRun knows their flow. Document in SPEC §11.P.Plot that the dispatcherHandle is the auto-done actor and recommend operators set a real handle on dispatch when accountability matters. Future enhancement: the NewPlanRun form can pre-populate dispatcherHandle from the logged-in user when warren has an auth surface.

Concurrent PlanRuns bound to the same Plot interleave per-child run_dispatched events in unpredictable order. Mitigation: Plot's append-only event log is event-id-keyed and idempotent on replay (per ../plot/SPEC.md §5), so events themselves are correct but ordering reflects wall-clock-insert order. For V1, accept this — operators rarely dispatch two concurrent PlanRuns against the same Plot. Document and file a follow-up if observed.

Plot index rebuild on first dispatch hot-path adds latency. Mitigation: Phase 1 already mitigates by triggering rebuild during projects/refresh (pl-2047 risk #3). The same rebuild covers PlanRun's first append. The POST /plan-runs handler's best-effort posture means the rebuild happens asynchronously to the row insert — operators get a 201 even if the index rebuild hasn't finished.

Coordinator reading planRun.plotId after the row has been cancelled (PlanRun cancellation race). Mitigation: POST /plan-runs/:id/cancel (added by warren-f923) transitions state to 'cancelled' but does NOT delete the row. The coordinator's listActive query returns ('queued','running') only; cancelled PlanRuns are no longer advanced. The auto-done transition only fires on the plan_succeeded arm which is unreachable from cancelled. No race.
acceptance: POST /plan-runs accepts an optional plot_id; row stores it; GET /plan-runs/:id and the list endpoint surface it.
POST /plan-runs with plot_id against a project where hasPlot=false rejects with code:'project_lacks_plot' and recoveryHint pointing at `plot init`; no row is inserted.
POST /plan-runs without plot_id against a project where hasPlot=false succeeds normally (the absence of plot_id means no plot involvement).
POST /plan-runs without plot_id against a project where hasPlot=true is byte-identical to the pl-a258 baseline (zero Plot leakage in the response payload and zero Plot writes).

A PlanRun with plot_id emits exactly one plan_run_dispatched event on the bound Plot at creation time, with actor=`user:<dispatcherHandle>` and payload {plan_run_id, plan_id, children_count}; Plot append failures emit a plan_run.plot_append_failed warren event without failing the POST.

Each child run dispatched by the coordinator for a Plot-bound PlanRun has PLOT_ID=<plot id> and PLOT_ACTOR=`agent:<agent-name>:<child-run-id>` in its sandbox env (asserted via printenv in scenario 27).
Each child run produces exactly one run_dispatched event on the Plot via the unchanged Phase 1 path (defaultPlotAppender), including the trivial-merge case where commitsAhead=0.

When the final child reaches `merged` and the coordinator transitions the PlanRun to `succeeded`, the bound Plot auto-transitions from `active` to `done` via setStatus('done') as `user:<dispatcherHandle>` — verified via the Plot's status field AND a `status_changed` event in the Plot's event log.

If the bound Plot is in any status other than `active` at plan-success time (drafting, ready, done, archived), setStatus is NOT called; a plan_run.plot_status_skipped warren event is logged for observability and the PlanRun's `succeeded` state stands.
If the auto-done setStatus throws (e.g. Plot file deleted, fs error), a plan_run.plot_auto_done_failed warren event is logged; the PlanRun is unaffected.
src/db/schema/drift.test.ts and src/db/migrations/migrations.test.ts pass after step 2; the postgres migration mirror exists.
scripts/acceptance/scenarios/27-plan-run-plot-roundtrip.ts passes against a real warren+burrow stack; scenarios 25 and 26 also still pass (no regressions); all are idempotent on repeat runs.
Single-run POST /runs and the existing PlanRun cancel/get/events handlers are byte-identical (their existing tests pass without modification).
warren/SPEC.md has a new subsection §11.P.Plot covering data flow, gating-stack, the plan_run_dispatched event, the auto-done transition contract, and the cross-repo plot-3e3d dependency.
warren/README.md and warren/CLAUDE.md mention the PlanRun + Plot composition as a feature that lights up when both .plot/ and .seeds/ are present.
CHANGELOG.md has a new entry; package.json + src/index.ts VERSION are dual-bumped consistently.
`bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` succeeds from the warren root with zero warnings (biome --error-on-warnings gate).

sections.context:
Phase 3 of warren-000b — the final and most user-visible chunk of warren's Plot adoption. Phase 1 (pl-2047) wired Plot into single-run dispatch with no UI change: project.hasPlot detection, runs.plot_id column, PLOT_ID/PLOT_ACTOR env injection at burrow.up, run_dispatched emission, reap-time event mirroring. Phase 2 (pl-7937 / warren-06dc) composed the same wiring onto PlanRun: plan_runs.plot_id, plan_run_dispatched emission, per-child PLOT_ID inheritance, auto-transition Plot → done on plan_succeeded. Both phases shipped with deliberate UI placeholders: PlanRunDetail.tsx already renders a dead Link to /plots/:id (mx-757be9), and NewPlanRun.tsx hides the Plot ID input when hasPlot is false (mx-be04a6). This plan makes those placeholders live and rebuilds the warren UI around Plot as the primary coordination unit.

Driven by the post-V1 direction in the Agentic Networks blog post (mx-0bd7e3, mx-90ad02): the unit users care about is no longer the run (orchestrator → worker) but the Plot (shared substrate where humans and agents coexist as peer nodes). Run-centric UIs reify orchestrator-above-worker hierarchy; Plot-centric UIs render the human as a peer alongside agents. Steering becomes the default mode, dispatch the special case. The CLAUDE.md standalone-path guarantee constrains the shift: a deployment with zero .plot/ projects must look byte-identical to today's run-centric UI, so the new surfaces gate on someProject.hasPlot && anyPlotExists at every entry point.

The substrate to build on already exists. src/plot-client/ exposes UserPlotClient with .list() / .query() / .rebuildIndex() / handle.append / .editIntent / .setStatus / .attach / .detach over @os-eco/plot-cli's PlotStore + SQLitePlotIndex (mx-bd4d67). Per-project .plot/ presence is surfaced as project.hasPlot from src/projects/refresh.ts (warren-4e20). runs.plot_id and plan_runs.plot_id columns + indexes are in place. The fire-and-log Plot-edge pattern (mx-82a120) is the documented posture for every server-side Plot write. resolveDispatcherHandle (mx-6a9788) gives a default 'operator' actor for write endpoints until warren grows real user auth. The pl-2047 risk #6 'live event gap' (events only visible after reap) carries into Phase 3 as a documented limitation — separate live-channel epic, not in scope here. Status-tag triggers (ready → planner agent) are explicitly deferred per the parent epic.
sections.approach:
Three sub-phases shipped behind step.blocks ordering inside a single plan. Each sub-phase is independently mergeable: 3a (steps 1-7) ships /plots list and create with both /runs and /plots side-by-side; 3b (steps 8-18) ships /plots/:id detail with three panels and the question-answer round-trip; 3c (steps 19-20) flips the default landing and ships the release.

Server surface mirrors the existing /runs and /plan-runs shape: a new src/plots/ module owns aggregation, a thin set of /plots and /plots/:id handlers in src/server/handlers.ts route through UserPlotClient (the facade enforces SPEC §6 ACL — mx-bd4d67 — so warren never constructs an agent-actor write to humans-only event types). Every Plot write endpoint accepts an optional dispatcher_handle the same way POST /runs does (mx-6a9788), defaulting to 'operator' when omitted or malformed. Aggregation across hasPlot projects parallelizes per-project SQLitePlotIndex queries and best-effort-rebuilds the index on miss (mirrors mx-239786). Read endpoints poll-only, matching RunDetail / PlanRunDetail cadence — the live channel from sandbox → UI stays deferred per pl-2047 risk #6.

UI is React + HashRouter (mx-159c0f) — /plots and /plots/:id live entirely on the client; no server route shadows them. tanstack-query handles caching with 5s staleTime (mx-268674 pattern). PlotDetail's three-panel layout (Intent, Substrate, ActivityFeed) reuses existing shadcn primitives in src/ui/src/components/ui/ — no new Tailwind tokens (mx-7fc92a). Optimistic UI on Intent edits, status transitions, question answers; failure restores draft. The 'Run plan' button composes onto pl-7937: it POSTs to /plan-runs with plot_id auto-filled, hitting the existing project_lacks_seeds → project_lacks_plot stacked-gate (mx-4b7ff8). The PlanRunDetail back-link (mx-757be9) flips from placeholder to live in step 17.

3c's default-landing flip is the only non-additive change. It gates on someProject.hasPlot && anyPlotExists (not just hasPlot — a project with an empty .plot/ dir shouldn't flip the entry), preserving CLAUDE.md's standalone path. /runs stays reachable below 'Plots' in the sidebar. Step 20 closes the loop with the full release flow: SPEC §11.O.Plot.UI subsection per the mx-49a44c five-thing template (gating contract, env/API contract, ACL surface, ASCII data-flow diagram, deferred-to-later-phases callouts), README features bullet, CHANGELOG entry, version bump 0.3.18 → 0.4.0 in package.json + src/index.ts (the two-place pin verified by .github/workflows/release.yml), ml records across ui/server/planning/acceptance, scenarios 25 + 27 + 28 + 29 all green, quality trinity (bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck) clean.

alternatives.name: Live Plot-event channel (SSE/WS) from sandbox to UI instead of polling
alternatives.rejected_because: pl-2047 risk #6 documents the live channel as a separate epic. JSONL-tail-at-reap is what .mulch/ and .seeds/ already do; the merge primitive is in place via mergePlot (mx-98e080). Polling matches Runs/PlanRunDetail and ships the UI faster. The live channel is additive and can land in a future epic without re-architecting Phase 3.

alternatives.name: Require ?project_id= on GET /plots/:id
alternatives.rejected_because: SQLitePlotIndex.query({plotId}) resolves the owning project from the plot id alone. Forcing the caller to know the project would leak warren's internal aggregation shape into the URL and complicate the PlanRunDetail back-link (mx-757be9), which only has plotId. Plot ids are globally unique within the deployment by construction; the aggregation handler resolves the project.

alternatives.name: Talk to PlotStore directly from handlers, skip the src/plot-client/ facade
alternatives.rejected_because: Same logic as pl-2047 alt #5. The facade narrows agent-actor writes to humans-only event types at compile time (HUMANS_ONLY_EVENT_TYPES pinned with 'as const satisfies' — mx-bd4d67). Bypassing it for read paths only would make the contract less legible and invite future drift. Cheap defense; mx-bd4d67 is the pinned pattern.

alternatives.name: Auto-deprecate /runs entirely in 3c — remove the route
alternatives.rejected_because: CLAUDE.md explicitly preserves the standalone path: 'a user with a GitHub URL and an Anthropic key can dispatch a run end-to-end with no other tooling.' Deployments with zero .plot/ projects need /runs to stay primary. Keeping /runs reachable behind a sidebar entry is the minimal shift; the default-landing flip is the user-visible part.

alternatives.name: Build a new POST /plots/:id/dispatch endpoint for the 'Run plan' button
alternatives.rejected_because: pl-7937 already shipped POST /plan-runs with plot_id support and the stacked gating (project_lacks_seeds before project_lacks_plot — mx-4b7ff8). Reusing it keeps the gating story single-sourced and avoids a parallel dispatch surface. The 'Run plan' button is a thin client-side wrapper over the existing endpoint with plot_id auto-filled.

alternatives.name: Status-tag triggers (drafting → ready spawns a planner agent that calls sd plan)
alternatives.rejected_because: Parent epic warren-000b explicitly says 'post-Plot-V1, defer; file follow-up when status-transition reliability is proven.' Including it here would expand scope past what 3a/3b/3c can ship and would couple the UI shift to a new agent dispatch path. File a follow-up seed after 3b lands and status-transition reliability has live operator data.

alternatives.name: Single mega-page combining list + detail in one /plots route
alternatives.rejected_because: Two routes is what Runs/PlanRuns / Projects use today and matches the mental model of 'click a row, drill in.' A combined view forces every Plot's full event log into the same response and breaks the polling cadence model. Two routes is also what mx-757be9 already wires (the PlanRunDetail back-link target is /plots/:id, not /plots?focused=:id).

alternatives.name: Embed a live PlanRun coordinator dashboard inside PlotDetail
alternatives.rejected_because: PlanRunDetail.tsx already exists and polls every 5s with PLAN_RUN_TERMINAL_STATES (mx-4c064b). Duplicating that surface inside PlotDetail would fork the polling logic; instead PlotDetail's 'Run plan' button routes to PlanRunDetail.tsx after dispatch, and PlanRunDetail.tsx's existing back-link (step 17) closes the loop.

steps.title: src/plots/: server-side Plot aggregation module — typed facade over UserPlotClient.list/query/rebuildIndex across hasPlot projects, returning PlotSummary[] {id, name, status, intent_goal_preview, attachments_count, last_event_ts, last_event_actor, project_id}. Parallel per-project queries; best-effort rebuildIndex+retry once on miss (mx-239786 pattern); 5s in-memory cache keyed by project_id. PlotResolver helper resolves owning project for a given plot_id by querying every hasPlot project's index. Unit tests pin the byte-identical-empty-array contract when zero projects have hasPlot=true.

GET /plots in src/server/handlers.ts + router registration in src/server/router.ts: optional ?status= filter (drafting|ready|active|done|archived); returns PlotSummary[] aggregated via src/plots/. Empty array (not 404) when no hasPlot projects exist. NDJSON-meta tagging not needed — list is a single response. Handler test pins the empty-deployments-return-empty-array contract.

POST /plots in src/server/handlers.ts: accepts {project_id, name?, intent?, dispatcher_handle?}; gated on project.hasPlot with typed ProjectLacksPlotError (mirrors §11.O / mx-d9d1c7 shape); opens a UserPlotClient against project.localPath/.plot, calls PlotStore.createPlot or equivalent via the facade, returns the new PlotSummary. dispatcher_handle resolution via resolveDispatcherHandle with 'operator' fallback (mx-6a9788). Failure to create is hard-rejected (no fire-and-log here — the user is waiting on the create result). Handler test covers the happy path + the no-.plot/ rejection.

risks: GET /plots aggregation cost across N hasPlot projects: SQLitePlotIndex.query is fast per-project but warren is fanning N queries on every list call. Step 1 must parallelize and apply a 5s in-memory cache keyed by project_id (mx-268674 ProjectDetail pattern). For deployments with >10 hasPlot projects, consider a per-project last_event_ts index and only re-query projects whose last_event_ts has advanced — defer the optimization unless the scenario 28 measurement shows it's needed.

Live-event gap (pl-2047 risk #6) surfaces visibly in ActivityFeed: events from a running agent only appear after reap completes. ActivityFeed must document this inline (e.g. a small 'Events update after each agent run completes' note in the empty state) and the SPEC subsection in step 20 must reiterate that the live channel is a separate epic. Don't let users expect live updates and silently get them via polling-after-reap.

No real user auth in warren: server-side UserPlotClient runs as user:operator default (mx-6a9788). Intent edits, status transitions, attachment changes, and question answers all attribute to 'operator' until warren grows a user-auth surface. Every write endpoint accepts an optional dispatcher_handle the same way POST /runs does — clients can plumb whatever principal they have. Don't over-engineer; 'operator' is the documented fallback.

Question-answer concurrency: two reviewers answering the same question_posed event in different sessions would both succeed against the Plot library (no library-level uniqueness guarantee). Step 12 must check at the handler edge — read the latest Plot, search for question_answered referencing the question_event_id, reject if present. Acceptance scenario 29 pins the already-answered rejection.

First-list SQLitePlotIndex absent: a project whose .plot/.index.db has never been built (gitignored, rebuildable) will fail step 1's per-project query on first call. Best-effort client.rebuildIndex() then retry once before failing the aggregation (mirrors mx-239786). If the project has many Plots the rebuild cost is noticeable on the dispatch hot path; consider triggering rebuild during projects/refresh on first detection of hasPlot, same lesson as pl-2047 risk #3.

HashRouter shadowing (mx-159c0f): /plots and /plots/:id are SPA-only routes. The matching server API namespace is /plots (already routed via src/server/handlers.ts). On hard reload the server sees /, returns index.html, and the SPA reconciles the hash route — no shadowing risk. sections.context: Warren's spawn flow (warren-e26f, src/runs/spawn.ts) constructs `{env: {PLOT_ID, PLOT_ACTOR}}` and forwards it to BurrowClient.burrowsUp on the burrow up POST body — expecting burrow to thread those values into the sandbox so the in-sandbox `plot` CLI can append events with the right actor and Plot id. Today burrow silently drops body.env at its handler edge (burrow-59cd), so PLOT_ID arrives empty inside the sandbox and the os-eco Plot integration's agent-side append path is half-disconnected. Acceptance scenario 25 (warren-4e06) detects this gap: the env-reaches-sandbox, workspace plot append, and plot.* mirror assertions are currently soft-skipped behind a conditional. Once burrow-59cd ships and is consumed here, those assertions flip to hard-required and the Plot integration is end-to-end honest for the first time. This is the warren-side companion to burrow-59cd; gated on that fix releasing in @os-eco/burrow-cli.
sections.reproduction: 1. Set `PLOT_ID=plot-abc` and dispatch a run against a project with `.plot/`. 2. Inside the spawned sandbox, run `printenv PLOT_ID`. Observed: empty. Expected: plot-abc. 3. Run `scripts/acceptance/scenarios/25-plot-dispatch-roundtrip.ts` (the scenario where this assertion lives) and see the env-reaches-sandbox / workspace-plot-append / plot.* mirror checks logged as 'soft-skipped pending burrow-a346' rather than asserted. The cross-repo gap is in burrow (burrow-59cd) but this seed tracks the warren-side bump + assertion flip that closes the gap from the consumer side.
sections.root_cause: The defect's source is in burrow, not warren — burrow's createBurrow handler at burrow/src/server/handlers.ts:404 omits body.env parsing (see burrow-59cd). Warren's send-side at BurrowClient.burrowsUp (src/burrow-client/client.ts) and the call site in src/runs/spawn.ts are both correct. The remaining warren work is consumption: bump @os-eco/burrow-cli to the version where burrow-59cd lands (double-pinned per the CLAUDE.md 'Relationship to burrow' convention — package.json + bun.lock + Dockerfile global install + the burrow runtime image, all in sync, mismatch is a no-op since Bun.spawn resolves ./node_modules/.bin/burrow before PATH). Then flip scenario 25's conditional assertions to unconditional so a future regression in the burrow contract fails CI loudly.
sections.approach: Single-PR consumer-side update gated on burrow-59cd merging and releasing to npm. (a) Bump @os-eco/burrow-cli in all four pin locations (package.json dep + dev-dep if present, bun.lock via `bun install`, Dockerfile global install line, and any composed image tag if present). (b) Locate scenario 25's soft-skipped assertions (search for `pending warren-a346` or `pending burrow-a346` in scripts/acceptance/scenarios/25-*.ts) and remove the conditional guards so they run unconditionally. (c) Run scenario 25 against a live warren+burrow stack to confirm green; if it fails, the burrow release is wrong or warren's send-side payload shape doesn't match what burrow now parses (parseEnvMap expects Record<string,string>). (d) Patch-bump warren and note the upgrade in CHANGELOG. Alternative rejected: keep the conditional in scenario 25 indefinitely with a comment — leaves the soft-skip as silent rot and any future burrow regression goes undetected.

sections.context: Warren's host-side Plot appenders (defaultPlotAppender in src/runs/spawn.ts, defaultPlanRunPlotAppender in src/plan-runs/plot-appender.ts, autoTransitionPlotToDone in src/plan-runs/plot-transition.ts) write to `project_clone/.plot/<id>.{events.jsonl,json}` WITHOUT committing — they call PlotHandle.append/setStatus which writes the file but doesn't run git. refreshProjectClone (src/projects/refresh.ts:139) runs `git reset --hard origin/<ref>` on every spawnRun and wipes those uncommitted edits before the next child dispatches. Observed in scenario 27 (warren-97a3): only the LAST child's run_dispatched line and the post-final-child status_changed line survive on disk; the plan_run_dispatched event and prior children's run_dispatched events are gone, and the auto-`done` status snapshot reverts to `active` on the next refresh. Scenario 27 currently works around this by tailing the events.jsonl every 100ms to accumulate the transient writes. Production impact: an operator inspecting `.plot/` at rest sees only the last surviving writes; the Plot status in the on-disk `.json` is not durable across PlanRuns or any subsequent spawnRun against the same project. This blocks the warren-on-warren dogfood story — without it, warren's own Plot history doesn't survive plan-runs against itself. Fixed via shape (b) per design discussion: preserve `.plot/` across the reset rather than committing it through reap (shape a, filed as a follow-up).
sections.reproduction: 1. Create a project with `.plot/` initialized and `hasPlot=true`. 2. Init a Plot in the project (`plot init` in the clone, or via warren's POST /plots). 3. Dispatch a plan-run with N>=2 children against a seeds plan in the project. 4. Watch `<project_clone>/.plot/<plot-id>.events.jsonl` while the plan-run executes — appends accumulate during a child's spawn (plan_run_dispatched at start, run_dispatched per child), then disappear at the next spawnRun's refreshProjectClone call. 5. After the plan-run completes, observe that the on-disk events.jsonl contains only the FINAL child's run_dispatched and the auto-`done` status_changed event; earlier events are gone. 6. Observe that the .json snapshot's status='done' written by autoTransitionPlotToDone reverts to 'active' if any further refreshProjectClone fires against the same clone. Scenario 27 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/27-plan-run-plot-roundtrip.ts) demonstrates this with its 100ms tail workaround that accumulates the transient writes into an in-memory log; remove the tail and the scenario assertions fail.
sections.root_cause: refreshProjectClone in src/projects/refresh.ts:139 calls `git reset --hard origin/<ref>` (or `git reset --hard <ref>` fallback) which discards ALL uncommitted changes in the working tree, including untracked files added since the last commit. The host-side Plot appenders write to `.plot/<id>.events.jsonl` and `.plot/<id>.json` in the working tree but never `git add` or `git commit` them — they rely on the working tree itself being durable, which it isn't across a hard reset. The reset is correct for the workspace-branch-on-each-child invariant (the agent's working tree must start clean) but is overbroad: it wipes warren-managed coordination state alongside agent detritus. Cross-cuts pl-2047 (Phase 1 host appender), pl-7937 (Phase 2 PlanRun appender + auto-done), and scenario 27 (which masks the bug with the 100ms tail workaround).
sections.approach: Shape (b) from the seed: preserve `.plot/` across the reset. In refreshProjectClone, after detecting `hasPlot` via the existing detectProjectFeatures (which already reads `.plot/`), snapshot the `.plot/` directory's contents to an out-of-tree temp location BEFORE the reset, run the reset, then restore. The snapshot uses fs.cp (or readdir+readFile loop for tighter control) to copy the events.jsonl and json files for each Plot id; restore writes them back to the same paths. Conflict resolution: on restore, if a path also exists in the freshly-reset tree (because the project repo committed an older version), the snapshot wins — host-side appender writes are append-only and strictly newer than anything committed in the project. The SQLite index at .plot/.index.db is derived state and is gitignored per ../plot/README.md; do not preserve it across the reset (let it rebuild via the existing rebuildIndex retry-once path, mx-239786). File a follow-up seed for shape (a) — commit `.plot/` through reap so it round-trips to origin and survives across machines — that's a separate, larger change. Alternative considered and rejected for now: (a) commit-through-reap delivers durability to origin but trivial-merge children with no agent commits would carry no Plot writes home; (c) out-of-tree side-table mirroring warren events is a new persistence surface that diverges Plot's on-disk shape from what `plot show` reads. Shape (b) is single-function, restores correctness today, and doesn't commit warren to a side-table.

steps.title: Snapshot+restore .plot/ in refreshProjectClone
steps.description: Modify src/projects/refresh.ts refreshProjectClone: before the `git reset --hard` block (line ~137), if `exists(path.join(localPath, '.plot'))`, snapshot the `.plot/` directory's tracked + untracked file contents (events.jsonl, json) to a temp dir under os.tmpdir() with a unique name. Skip `.plot/.index.db` (derived, gitignored). After the reset completes (and the user.name/user.email --unset-all loop, line ~155), restore every snapshotted file by writing it back to its original path under `.plot/`; create parent dirs as needed. On any error during snapshot or restore, surface a typed error (define `PlotPreservationError extends ProjectUnavailableError` in src/projects/errors.ts, or reuse ProjectUnavailableError with a clear message) — do NOT silently swallow, because data loss is the whole point of this fix. The snapshot temp dir is cleaned up in a finally block.

steps.title: Unit test: refresh preserves .plot/ appends
steps.description: Add tests to src/projects/refresh.test.ts (or create if it doesn't exist) using the existing fixture pattern. Three cases: (a) a project with `.plot/<id>.events.jsonl` containing 3 appended events survives a refreshProjectClone call — assert byte-equality after refresh; (b) a project with `.plot/<id>.json` containing `{status: 'done'}` (where origin's committed version has `status: 'active'`) survives — the local-side `done` wins on restore; (c) a project WITHOUT `.plot/` is byte-identical to today's refresh behavior — no extra fs calls, no error. Mock git via the existing spawn-fn injection; mock fs only where needed for the snapshot/restore paths. Idempotency: running refreshProjectClone twice in a row with no intervening writes is a no-op.

steps.title: Remove scenario 27's 100ms tail workaround
steps.description: In scripts/acceptance/scenarios/27-plan-run-plot-roundtrip.ts, locate the 100ms tail loop that accumulates transient `.plot/<id>.events.jsonl` writes into an in-memory log (search for `setInterval(100` or `tail` or comments referencing this workaround). Remove the loop. Re-source the assertions from the on-disk events.jsonl directly at the end of the plan-run. The plan_run_dispatched event, per-child run_dispatched events, and the final status_changed event should all be present in the durable on-disk file. Update inline comments to remove references to the workaround. Run scenario 27 against a live stack to confirm green.

steps.title: Follow-up seed: shape (a) commit-through-reap
steps.description: Create a new seed in warren's .seeds/ titled 'Commit .plot/ through reap so Plot history reaches origin' with type=task priority=medium. Description: shape (b) preserves .plot/ across refreshes in a single working tree, but doesn't round-trip Plot writes to origin — so a fresh clone on another machine has no history of warren's host-side appends. Shape (a) layers on top: at reap time (src/runs/reap.ts), after the agent's workspace branch is pushed, also stage and commit any .plot/<id>.{events.jsonl,json} changes warren made (filter to host-side-modified files via the snapshot diff or by tracking append targets in spawn.ts) on a separate commit (or amended into the same workspace push) so they ride along. Link to this seed and to the plot mulch records (mx-a224cf, mx-c4307b). Do NOT implement here — scope of warren-6f25 ends with shape (b).

After autoTransitionPlotToDone fires, the on-disk `.plot/<id>.json` reports `status: "done"` and that value is durable across a subsequent refreshProjectClone call against the same project clone — the snapshot's `done` wins over origin's older `active`.
